programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: All you need in the kitchen for your precision slicing and dicing of fruits and vegetables. Including three cutting inserts, a finger protector, blade holder and a free docking station.

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Stewart is selected to be part of a group of men known as LA's most eligible bachelors, but is horrified to discover the chosen few are being systematically killed - and after examining puncture marks on the victims' necks, Sloan has to explore some unorthodox theories

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan links a suspicious suicide to a dating agency, prompting Jack to go undercover to investigate - but he must take the deception further than anticipated to crack the case. Mystery drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Scott Baio

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble inadvertently happens to be in the background when a sidewalk photographer snaps a picture of some newlyweds

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry deals with swapped identities, embezzlement and a shady insurance investigator when he is called upon to defend a woman accused of an ice-pick murder.

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: The lawman steps in to help a champion kickboxer who has been framed for the murder of his manager

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Criminals kidnap a bank employee's baby to force her to provide them with inside information on how to commit a robbery - but two are killed in the ensuing raid and the third escapes without revealing the youngster's location. The team immediately mounts a search, but work is hampered by relentless rain and an unexpected terrorist response drill

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Naval intelligence ensign is killed in an unusual car accident. Gibbs and his team are reunited with old friend Ned Dorneget, who is now working with NCIS cyber operations, and NSA attorney Jake Malloy to investigate the case, and discover a conspiracy involving terrorist recruitment on the internet

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when an Islamic terrorist group in Iraq steals a truck with $5million and track the money back to America. The agents learn the cash has been used to set up a bank robbery intended as a ruse to allow a bank employee to transfer $100million of the terrorists' money that has been frozen by the government in an offshore bank. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: When Kimble asks a university law professor whether a new trial would help him, the professor has his students stage a mock trial using the real transcript of the case.

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Part two of two. Stone tries to salvage his prosecution against the Masucci family, but faces the prospect of losing his case for good when Beigel - himself facing bribery charges - appears unwilling to turn against his brother-in-law

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Return of the American drama following the work of the Naval Criminal Investigative Service. Gibbs and McGee travel to Russia to escort a computer expert connected to classified intel. However, the mission is compromised and they are forced to go off the grid while being hunted by Russian mercenaries. Meanwhile, the team in DC frantically tries to locate the missing pair. Mark Harmon and Sean Murray star

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: An abused lady nearly quits becoming a Texas Ranger after receiving threats from her ex-husband

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The murder of a coastguard officer on board an abandoned cargo ship draws the team's attention to a family of Lebanese refugees hoping to find a new home in America. Meanwhile, Tony and McGee try to find the perfect woman for Gibbs. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Calvin Lee, an executive naval officer, appears to commit suicide by hanging himself from a public road bridge. When examining CCTV footage of the incident, Sam and Callen discover the dead man tossed away a small object before jumping to his death, and when they learn the late lieutenant commander had been working on a sensitive project involving new cloaking technology for submarines, they immediately take an interest in the item. The investigation draws the team into the furtive world of LA's Chinese community, where they discover Lee might have been harbouring some personal as well as professional secrets. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a Navy Lieutenant who has been murdered while on his way to a meeting with the President, and must ascertain whether the killing was part of a recent spate of muggings in the area - or a targeted hit to keep information secret. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Special Agent Ned Dorneget of the NCIS cyber division joins the team to further their investigation into The Calling, the insidious terror network radicalising youngsters via the internet. When it emerges young suicide bomber Bradley was not, in fact, affiliated with The Calling, Gibbs and his colleagues realise they might be back to square-one. Determined to bring the organisation down, the agents pursue two new leads, an arms dealer who may be supplying The Calling with their signature German S-mines, and another boy given a deadly mission by the terrorists. Guest starring Matt Jones

Crime drama, guest starring Cheryl Ladd

programme.desc: A naval officer involved in an illegal underground street race is killed when his vehicle explodes. The team's investigations reveal he was carrying classified equipment in relation to his work on a new form of battery that the military had hoped to adopt, and that the explosion - captured on CCTV - is not in accordance with that expected of normal fuel. Meanwhile, Callen latest driving misdemeanour means he must attend traffic school. Chris O'Donnell stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The death of Special Agent Ned Dorneget hits the team hard, and when Gibbs receives a visit from his late colleague's mother - CIA Officer Joanna Teague - he must decide whether to let her help with the investigation into the Calling. Meanwhile, Gibbs hopes his young charge Luke, formerly one of the Calling's radicalised footsoldiers, might be key to bringing the organisation down. However, when the terrorists discover where their defecting comrade has taken refuge, the abduct him and flee to Iraq. The team follows hot on their heels, but it seems Luke's ties to the Calling might be stronger than anyone at NCIS ever anticipated. Naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with guest Mimi Rogers

programme.desc: Homeland Security employee Victor Sterling mysteriously disappears and his car is found at a drive-through restaurant that becomes the scene of a double murder. American naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, Joe Spano, Tom Gallop and Melinda McGraw

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine gunnery sergeant Wendell Dobbs is gunned down in a drive-by shooting by assailants wielding AK-47s, and the team suspects the victim may have been involved in illegally importing automatic weapons into the country and supplying them to local gangs. Sam goes undercover to find the truth after learning Dobbs gave up his combat post to mentor his younger brother, who had been hanging out with a gang. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The NCIS team is assigned to a joint terrorism task force by the Secretary when a Moscow-born scientist who it is believed may have been targeted by the Russians for selling state secrets, is found dead. The trail appears to lead to a friend of Sofia's, another scientist who worked for Russia and who may have succumbed to professional jealousy. However, when she too is found dead, the team discovers the hallmarks of Sergei Mishnev, the terrorist who nearly killed Gibbs. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.desc: Diana breaks Tom out of hospital, Harvey decides getting married will help him secure membership of the golf club, and Geoffrey believes his wife is evil

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: A friend's lack of interest in life prompts Compo, never one to shy away from danger, to volunteer to test Wesley's heavy-duty sucker and blower, which can only mean disaster. Roy Clarke's comedy, with Bill Owen, Gordon Wharmby and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives return home after their trip to South Africa, to find Nonnatus House has come under new management. The formidable Sister Ursula (Harriet Walter) has taken charge and introduced some drastic changes to the way the clinic is run, which Sister Julienne, bound by her vow of obedience, is powerless to oppose. The midwives come to the aid of a victim of domestic violence whose husband is about to be released from prison, and who risks losing everything if she seeks a divorce. Sister Mary Cynthia suffers further anxiety attacks, and Patsy receives distressing news about her father

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Ursula insists the midwives do not become emotionally involved with their patients, but her harsh new approach proves hard to cope with in the face of two devastating cases. A father-to-be is badly injured in an explosion, leaving him unable to work to support his family, and a couple with dwarfism expecting their first child are warned that their child may be stillborn, a situation that leaves Patsy confronted with one of the hardest decisions of her life

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad reopens the investigation into an actress's disappearance when clips of her being assaulted in a 1990 movie are posted on the internet. Director Don Maddox features in the footage, but when questioned, he appears to have problems distinguishing fact from fiction. A potential witness is then found dead, and Brian and Jack are seconded to the unit handling the case, only to find Emily Driscoll (Dennis Waterman's daughter Hannah) is heading the operation. Imogen Stubbs and Sandra Dickinson also guest star

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Double bill. A rising star in the Essex police force is found beaten and shot in his burnt-out car in what proves to be an emotional case for his colleagues. The team helps DCI Jim Sullivan, the detective charged with bringing the killer to justice, and it isn't long before suspicion falls on Dean Fallon - a local drug dealer and the son of a known crime boss. But when he proves to have a solid alibi, the investigation shifts closer to home. Starring Emilia Fox and David Caves, with James Alexandrou and Jason Watkins

Paul Bradley stars, with Amanda Mealing, Robert Powell, Luke Roberts, Gillian Bevan, Keeley Forsyth, Jack O'Connor and Paul McNeilly

programme.desc: Stabbing victim Nikki recovers and returns to work, but it soon becomes clear she remains haunted by the ordeal. programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The government plans to close down small hospitals in favour of larger establishments. Dr Turner and Shelagh prepare for an inspection that will decide the fate of Nonnatus House - but the staff is shocked to find Sister Ursula has no intention of helping to save the maternity home. Barbara comes to the aid of a woman from the Chinese community who is struggling to get on with her mother-in-law, and whose baby is seriously ill

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A woman expecting her third child realises she cannot afford to care for her baby, so makes the painful decision to give it away to her wealthy but childless cousin. Shelagh befriends the woman in the next hospital bed and Dr Turner confides in Timothy about his worst fears. Sister Julienne looks for a new recruit to join the midwives, and the community prepares to celebrate the opening of a new park

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad reinvestigates the death of a timeshare magnate, previously thought to have committed suicide, when a reformed call girl claims she was with him the night he died and saw someone force their way into his house as she left. It then emerges the deceased's widow has since fallen on hard times after being swindled by her late husband's business partner - who is connected to a notorious criminal. Guest starring Shane Richie, Naomi Bentley and Claire Rushbrook

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of a DJ leads Nikki to question her judgement in a strikingly similar case from her past and she brings the potential mistake to the attention of her colleagues, but the news threatens to have repercussions for the family of the deceased, the police and the Lyell Centre. programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ricky sets out to impress with a special supper at the bedsit. Sharon is depressed when Grant hosts a very different kind of party upstairs at the Vic

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Pauline and Arthur are snubbed by Nellie, who spends Christmas with Jules. Cindy uses Gita as an alibi and Pat and Carol are competing to host Christmas dinner

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Will pays dearly for the satisfaction of punching Matt's lights out when Gina gives him a ferocious dressing down, the injured party files a charge and Emma starts to feel sorry for her ex anyway. To make matters worse, he's ruled himself out of the boxing tournament, leaving Dan to fly the flag for Sun Hill. Sam is shocked at Stuart's ruthless treatment of a suspect - a development not lost on Phil Hunter

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Connie returns as the team is holding a remembrance service, but finds Darwin understaffed and in chaos, and begins to stamp her authority in a bid to restore efficiency. Chrissie finds her first day back at work tough, while a dishevelled Donna arrives late for her shift - but rather than receiving a dressing-down, she discovers she has been nominated for the Nurse of the Year award

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Lara's day of reckoning arrives as she appears in court charged with manslaughter and Duffy is shocked when her mother is rushed into A&E suffering from what appears to be an epileptic fit - but the condition turns out to be much more sinister. Meanwhile, a new face in the department puts Simon and Dillon in a spin

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Feelings run high in the community when a 12-year-old suffers gunshot wounds but refuses to say who is responsible, leaving Adam to face the wrath of the estate residents. Lewis's rash decision to enter a squat puts Roger in a difficult situation, as he struggles to overcome the effects of post-traumatic stress. Will gets further encouragement from Emma, despite her ex's refusal to let her go

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ricky sets out to impress with a special supper at the bedsit. Sharon is depressed when Grant hosts a very different kind of party upstairs at the Vic

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Pauline and Arthur are snubbed by Nellie, who spends Christmas with Jules. Cindy uses Gita as an alibi and Pat and Carol are competing to host Christmas dinner

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Hi-Ho makes a shocking decision, Adam gets a nasty surprise when the police investigation takes an unexpected turn, and Recall is surprised by Ben's news

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Andrew stops dithering and proposes to Erica, but his mind is soon distracted from romance by the plight of a priest suffering from a mystery illness. While he's wondering what course of action to take, Nick continues his scheming and David becomes increasingly concerned about Clare's mental state, fearing she's about to be overtaken by manic depression again. Gary Mavers, Saskia Wickham and Adrian Lukis star

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: The body of a woman drowned in the bath prompts a tricky murder investigation for DS Diamond, whose inquiries are further hampered by Paige's attitude to the elderly suspect. Crime drama, starring Nigel Havers and Nicola Cowper

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon develops a disturbing aversion to work, while Dorien is disgusted by Tracey's amazing ability to attract men, a skill seemingly unhindered by her pregnancy. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Bayview prepares for a royal visit, and while Tom is thrilled at the prospect, Diana struggles to contain her obvious displeasure. Meanwhile, slow-on-the-uptake Jane starts to suspect Harvey is being unfaithful and wonders whether to look for someone new. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene and Leclerc escape from the Communist Resistance, the Colonel, Gruber, Bertorelli and Leclerc are held ransom by Denise, and Michelle takes advantage of the situation

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Truly decides to put his extensive police training to good use by roping Compo and Clegg into his newly formed active rescue team, and finds there is only one thing stopping them becoming heroes - the lack of a casualty. Long-running comedy, starring Frank Thornton

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A distant cousin of Fred's who has Down's syndrome is taken in by the Buckles following the death of his protective mother, but Violet struggles to cope and Shelagh is forced to make other arrangements for his care. New recruit Valerie prepares for her first day at Nonnatus House, and Trixie comes to the aid of a pregnant woman who is in urgent need of dental treatment to avoid an infection. The nurses discover what has happened to Sister Mary Cynthia, a revelation that has distressing consequences for Sister Monica Joan

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: While the world anxiously awaits the resolution of the Cuban Missile Crisis, Valerie treats a pregnant Somali woman who has undergone female genital circumcision. The Nonnatus Team arrange for her to go into hospital, but she responds by going into hiding, and the situation is complicated further when it emerges that the woman's younger sister will soon go through the same process. Sister Mary Cynthia's depression is treated with electroconvulsive therapy, which she hopes will restore her faith

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Gerry celebrates his birthday at a comedy club, where a controversial act comes under fire from a group of hecklers. Sandra discovers the most vocal member of the gang is the niece of a political activist who went missing 25 years previously after waging a campaign against the comedian. Agreeing to reinvestigate the disappearance, the squad discovers that the activist and her boyfriend had infiltrated an extreme right-wing group with links to the dangerous criminal who murdered Jack's wife. Guest starring Phil Daniels, Joe Absolom and John Henshaw

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: The murder of Amir Aziz, a prominent anti-fundamentalist Muslim, drags the Lyell team into the murky world of hate-crime and counter-terrorism. Initially thought to be the work of a EDL member whose son was killed by the Taliban, the team begin to suspect the killing is connected to Sarah Begovic, a young woman who left the UK to relocate to Syria, but who recently returned home in secret with her six-month-old son. Feature-length episode of the crime drama, starring Richard Lintern

programme.desc: Chef Bryn Williams ends his French journey in Nice, with Edward Jenkins who is originally from Ferryside. They visit Liberation market - which is full of fish and Mediterranean vegetables - and sample the best of Nice's street food, such as crêpes and fried courgette flowers. Bryn also cooks red mullet and fennel on the barbecue and prepares a classic tuna Niçoise salad and a Bouillabaisse fish soup

programme.title: Heno
programme.desc: Magazine programme, featuring stories from all over Wales

programme.title: Mamwlad
programme.desc: The author and business consultant profiles one final remarkable woman, celebrating the life and work of Barry-born composer Grace Williams with help from the National Youth Orchestra of Wales. Ffion meets some of Williams' relatives and neighbours, who discuss the processes by which the renowned composer completed her compositions, and explores some of the most colourful elements of her history - including friendships with influential contemporaries, a failed love affair, and her decision to refuse an honour from the Queen

programme.title: Newyddion S4C a'r Tywydd

programme.title: Prynhawn Da

programme.desc: News and weather

programme.title: Huw Edwards a'r Cymry Estron

programme.title: Olobobs
programme.desc: Some giant purple shellbobs are brought down to size Gee-ceffyl

programme.title: Hafod Haul

programme.title: Y Doniolis

programme.title: Cer I Greu
programme.desc: Huw sets the crew a challenge to create a 'flickbook', a little book that brings pictures to life

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda

programme.title: Larfa
programme.desc: The crazy characters consider the life of a rat

programme.title: Y Ty Cymreig
programme.desc: Aled Samuel and Dr Greg Stevenson explore the architectural history of the houses of Meirionnydd. They visit Aber Artro Hall near Harlech in Plas y Dduallt, Tan y Bwlch, Yr Ysgwrn farmhouse in Trawsfynydd and Crogen near Corwen

programme.title: Gêm Gartre
programme.desc: Owain Gwynedd hosts the fun sports quiz, which pits super fans against stars from the sporting world and other famous faces. At the end of the programme, the winner will aim to win up to a thousand pounds for a charity of their choice

programme.title: Golff: Pencampwriaeth Agored Cymru

programme.title: Cymru O'r Awyr

programme.title: Stori Pêl-Droed Cymru

programme.title: Gwesty Aduniad

programme.title: Low Box
programme.desc: Miriam Drott, Dafydd Brown and Dic Gerstic Bach pit Manitou against Merlo in a head-to-head competition to decide which is the better of the two. Plus, Rhydian and Eilir bid to be crowned the 2013 Welsh Melons Champion

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Sally Nugent

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Yana the tiger cracks a canine tooth and it must be removed, but it is not a simple procedure and the anaesthetic does not go to plan. Kate Humble and Ben Fogle present

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin explore a two-bed terrace in Salford, a three-bed semi in Derbyshire and a one-bedroom flat in south London

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, following the work of those providing emergency and medical care to the Shetland Islands' 23,000 residents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Agricultural Showground on Anglesey, where antique experts Roo Irvine and Richard Madley guide two teams as they search for items of interest and value to take to auction. Away from the contest, Charlie also meets a familiar face with a passion for Georgian glass

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Sabine suffers an indignity at a gig, and Shannon reacts badly to rejection

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: TV doctors David Bull, Ellie Cannon, Linda Papadopoulos, Ranj Singh and Javid Abdelmoneim take on the general-knowledge champions to win a cash prize for charity. Jeremy Vine asks the questions

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is in Cornwall, on hand to help a couple from London make their first joint property purchase with a budget of £750,000. Margherita also makes time to help out the head gardener at Trebah, one of the Great Gardens of Cornwall

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: A window cleaner, a children's party entertainer and a train supervisor compete to prove their profession is the toughest, with each spending a day working at the other's jobs

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell go in search for items in Cumbria, setting out from Cockermouth and heading towards an auction near Kendal

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball presents the first visit of the week to the studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Nazanin Zaghari-Ratcliffe has been held prisoner in Iran for more than four years after being convicted of spying charges. Nobody knows how many other British citizens have been imprisoned by the Iranian authorities, because the UK government refuses to say. Reporter Darragh MacIntyre meets the families of some of those who have been arbitrarily detained and asks whether the payment of a historic debt could set them free.

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Linda Henry and Luisa Bradshaw-White, aka sisters Shirley and Tina Carter, who talk about their characters' most memorable moments and revealing some of their best behind-the-scenes stories. Linda chats about how fans react to seeing 'Shirley' in the street, Luisa recalls her own brush with musical superstardom, and the pair explain how a tender scene quickly became a riot of laughter and chaos every time the cameras cut

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Learning that their posh neighbours the Beg-Chetwyndes have an ancient claim over their land, Mike and Alison throw an upmarket dinner party to help resolve a thorny issue. But with the ghosts her only guide to high-class decorum, the evening starts badly and only gets worse - thanks to Mary's woeful catering, Mike's clueless deal-making and Robin's raucous celebration of the lunar eclipse. Elsewhere, the spooks become obsessed with a TV sitcom. Comedy, guest starring Geoff McGivern and Sophie Thompson

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: Maan's disgrace has a disastrous effect on the Kapoor family, but when the bad press is used against his father in the general election campaign, it results in an unexpected gesture from an old friend. Following the revelation of further interference from her meddling family and emboldened by casting her vote in India's first national democratic election, Lata makes a decision about her future. Concluding part of Andrew Davies' adaptation of Vikram Seth's novel, starring Tanya Maniktala

programme.title: Famalam
programme.desc: The comedy sketch show returns, pulling back the curtain on interracial adverts and discovering what happens when someone reveals spoilers for a TV series. The E19 posse do their bit for the environment, there is an episode of Jamaican Countdown, and the African aunties learn their nephew's secret. Starring Vivienne Acheampong and Samson Kayo

programme.title: Canada's Drag Race
programme.desc: The Handmaid's Tale star Amanda Brugel is the guest host as the contestants are paired up with newcomers from LGBTQI charity Rainbow Railroad for a makeover challenge

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor David Tennant takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz from April 2019, with Conservative MP Johnny Mercer and comedian Zoe Lyons joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble meets an ostrich that thinks it is a zebra, Ben Fogle takes a test to identify all the park's guinea pigs by name, and Jean Johansson watches lions climbing trees

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets a couple renovating a home in Dorset, Martel Maxwell visits a mid-terrace in Greater Manchester and Dion Dublin meets a first-time developer in Ellesmere Port

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A farmer and his daughter are both rushed to A&E with injuries just as their family is busy with sheep-shearing season, and a crofter has to have a kidney removed

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Stephanie Connell help two teams scour Malvern Antiques and Collectors Fair to pick up items to sell at auction, while host Eric Knowles finds out more about a local Bargain Hunt veteran

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: A familiar face needs Claudia's help, and Mich is shamed when Sabine shares too much in class

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team made up of antiques experts takes on the general knowledge champions, as Raj Bisram, David Harper, Caroline Hawley, Gary Pe and Danny Sebastian hope to win a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a rural property shopper who has £250,000 to buy her ideal country home in north Wales and has drafted in her best friend for support. Along the way, the host takes a historic train ride along the beautiful coastline and meets the volunteer train driver who is helping keep Wales's railway heritage on track

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: A plumber, a tour guide and a dog groomer compete to prove their profession is the toughest, with each having to spend a day working at the other's jobs

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Steph Connell has a birthday surprise for Phil Serrell as they travel from Dumfries and Galloway to Edinburgh, stopping to look for gold in the highest village in the country

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Michael Ball present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Lorraine Stanley, Jessica Plummer and Toby-Alexander Smith to relive some of their characters' most iconic moments, share stories and reveal stuff that is normally kept under wraps

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Part one of two. Michael Mosley and Mehreen Baig investigate the lack of regulation in the cosmetic surgery industry. The programme examines how potentially risky surgeries have been replaced by non-invasive procedures like injectable fillers and fat freezing, and puts the science and safety of these new approaches to the test. The first episode focuses on facial procedures

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder follows the story of his grandfather who survived the Holocaust, hearing first-hand testimony of the horror of Nazi forced-labour camps as well as of the hope offered by a new life in Lake Windermere. Investigating the dark mystery surrounding his great-grandfather leads Robert to a small town in Latvia, where he uncovers a story of mental illness and trauma that will ultimately, he hopes, lay some ghosts to rest.

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Reflecting on the changing state of the nation as lockdown draws to an end, Michelle is joined by guest RuPaul, The Vivienne and Rylan Clark-Neal, organises a surprise Strictly Come Dancing reunion, and has an unexpected visit from eldest daughter Lillie

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce looks for a way to better cope with her anxiety, attending a yoga class with a difference and discovering the benefits of performing random acts of kindness to strangers. She also seeks the advice of actress-turned-blogger Holly Matthews, who has learned to find peace following a terrible tragedy in her life

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this episode, the contestants include a pest controller, a slam poet and a train signaller

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: The park sees the formation of its first ever all female wolf pack, and Ben gets a peek at some incredible footage of male koala, Burke

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a property in Birmingham with some mesmerising wallpaper and a south Wales house that has her dancing with joy. Meanwhile, Dion Dublin is in Liverpool to see a place that has him a bit confused

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: An oil rig worker from Tyne and Wear is rushed to the A&E department of Gilbert Bain Hospital with a suspected stroke

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp oversees proceedings as Philip Serrell and Catherine Southon help two teams scour Exeter's Westpoint Arena for items to take to auction in Ottery St Mary

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Hamid demands Kam come clean at school, and the proposed future for the Towers riles the residents. Starring Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team of TV news presenters takes on general knowledge champions the Goliaths, as Babita Sharma, Charlie Stayt, Sally Magnusson, Martine Croxall and Clive Myrie set out to secure a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton goes house hunting with a couple who want their £950,000 budget to buy them a family home in the West Sussex countryside that will give them a better work-life balance. While there, the presenter heads to the seaside town of Worthing, and finds out how one of the oldest working cinemas in the country has been saved from redevelopment

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Tree surgeon Olly, sandwich factory supervisor Ruth and zookeeper Charlotte compete, taking turns at doing one another's jobs before one of them walks away with the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell head to an auction in Hamilton. Steph comes across a chic French sign, but Phil is distracted by the local cuisine

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Following the life of 57-year-old Lorraine, a tribute act to the American pop star, Pink, exploring what it takes to spend your life pretending to be someone else

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a rusty toy pedal car that is missing its steering wheel, a Victorian stamp perforating machine, a bronze statue of a dagger-wielding assassin by leading French artist Claire Colinet, and a baize-covered card table dating from the 1940s that has sentimental value for its owner

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: When his father misses an appointment with his mental health worker, 25-year-old Oli calls Gloucestershire Police, concerned about the whereabouts of his father, Michael, who suffers from paranoid schizophrenia and is thought to have stopped taking his medication. With no sign of Michael overnight, police launch an appeal for information, and CCTV searches of the town reveal that Michael got into a taxi two nights before and was taken to a high point in the Cotswold's. Officers prepare for a major search operation, but looking for someone hiding in a heavily wooded area presents them with major challenges

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: Supernatural drama set in an alternate America, following three young witches from basic training in combat magic into early deployment on the military frontline to fight against a dangerous rebel group called The Spree. Starring Taylor Hickson, Jessica Sutton and Ashley Nicole Williams

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: The unit travels to Salem Town for an annual witch celebration, but an unexpected disturbance throws the proceedings into chaos. Meanwhile, Tally wonders if she made the right choice to embark on a military career. Supernatural drama, starring Taylor Hickson and Jessica Sutton

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on further highlights from 15 years of the show, remembering some of the most entertaining team challenges - and how different dynamics can lead to either success or chaos in the kitchen. They also revisit a 2015 semi-final task which saw Sheree Murphy, Scott Maslen, Rylan Clark-Neal, Sam Nixon, Chesney Hawkes, Mica Paris, Kimberly Wyatt and Tom Parker thrown together in a Victorian-style village, using only ingredients widely available during that era

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Climbing guide Jack, master tea-taster Sebastian and luxury couture retailer Onita go head to head in a bid to prove their job is the hardest and claim a £1,000 prize. Day one is steep challenge for Onita as she finds out how many times she's expected to climb one of London's most iconic tourist attractions. The next day sees Jack pushed for time as he grapples with the complexity of grading and blending tea leaves. On the final day, Jack and Sebastian try to master the science of window dressing and selling high-end women's clothing in a luxury boutique

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley and Ashley John-Baptiste discuss the personal and social benefits of spreading happiness to others, from video games in church to spending a day as a clown

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Mozart's situation improves after she is let back into the gang, but the Whiskers suffer a tragedy when they battle with the Commandos

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's 2,500-mile rail tour of south-east Asia reaches Malaysia. On the island of Penang, he traces the origins of the former British colony in Georgetown, rides one of the world's steepest funicular railways and is enchanted by exotic specimens at Malaysia's first butterfly sanctuary. Travelling on the nation's hi-tech railway network Michael visits its wettest town, Taiping, and learns how the discovery of tin made this land a prized possession for Britain. Next stop is the regal town of Kuala Kangsar, home to the Sultan of Perak and to one of Malaysia's most prestigious schools

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Evans family from Kent sample life in Brisbane, with mother-of-four Sue hoping to convince her husband and eldest daughter that the move is just what they need

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Miranda Krestovnikoff and Simon King join team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke on the panel show that tests wildlife knowledge, with Kate Humble asking the questions

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: George tells Sarah she must be clever about how to approach the woman she suspects of being a former concentration camp guard, while Douglas realises that his condition is terminal

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter V, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Incredible Medicine: Dr Weston's Casebook
programme.desc: Gabriel Weston continues to unlock the secrets of the human body, hearing the stories of a man who fixed his own heart and a girl whose immune system attacked her own brain. Dr Weston also hears the remarkable story of a man who can taste words, and how his condition is helping develop new ways for blind people to navigate and recognise colours

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates this documentary following a couple of mountain lion families in Wyoming's Rocky Mountains. The dramatic story of two mothers struggling to raise their cubs is giving scientists an invaluable insight into the behaviour of these elusive predators

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Anita Manning value items brought to Blackburn Cathedral, while Paul Martin learns about how mill workers in the town used to be woken by a human alarm clock

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angellica Bell, Gyles Brandreth, Scarlett Moffatt and Iain Stirling test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: Gardening Together with Diarmuid Gavin
programme.desc: The horticultural designer encourages people across Ireland to get out in their gardens, taking calls, answering questions and offering a range of practical tips. He helps design a garden for Mary Fitzpatrick in Co Wicklow and creates a sunken fire pit and seating area in his own patch

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Highlights of the fourth day of the Third Test, taking place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton, as the teams looked to conclude the series on a high

programme.title: Tom Kerridge: Lose Weight for Good
programme.desc: The chef recruits and mentors a group of struggling dieters, offering them advice on ways they can lose weight with long-term results. After losing over 12 stone himself through a combination of diet and exercise, Tom uses his own personal experiences to help the 13 dieters reduce their calorie intake, demonstrating how they can create their own simple, low-calorie dishes. Meeting the group for the first time, Tom lays out what they can expect from their weight-loss journey, and begins with tackling the issue of comfort eating, a problem that many of the group struggle with

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The seventh first-round match sees four students from the University of Bristol pit their wits against a team from Corpus Christi College, Oxford in a bid to reach the second stage of the quiz. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: The Unbelievable Story of Carl Beech
programme.desc: Vanessa Engle's documentary telling the story of the man who claimed he had been sexually abused by a group of prominent men in the 1970s and '80s, naming Jimmy Savile, ex-prime minister Edward Heath and former home secretary Leon Brittan as among those involved. The film is constructed using Beech's own words, taken from media and police interviews and from his blogs, and there are interviews with those who were close to the story at the time, including his ex-wife and Diana Clemetson, the widow of Lord Brittan

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and regular panellist Hugh Dennis look back on another eventful week in news and politics, with the help of fellow comedians Angela Barnes, Gary Delaney, Ed Gamble, Kerry Godliman and Nish Kumar. Stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Vienna, the city that once ran the Austro-Hungarian Empire and continues to be home to comfort dishes such as tafelspitz and goulash

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Michael Mosley investigates recent scientific research that could change the way people exercise, including a study that suggests many could benefit from just three minutes of high-intensity activity a week. He also discovers the health benefits of seemingly innocuous actions, such as walking and fidgeting, and learns why some people do not respond to exercise at all

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani are in Northumberland in the coastal village and ancient English royal kingdom of Bamburgh. Matt discovers what it takes to safeguard the castle from the elements, while Anita finds out about the secrets of bones dating back to the seventh century. Tom Heap investigates the new trend to escape the city for the country, and Adam Henson goes head to head with his actor neighbour Robert Llewellyn to discover how green machines size up to traditional diesel

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: A metal sculptor, a stained-glass designer and a textile artist pitch ideas for a Derby couple's wedding, with only two being made before the buyers choose their favourite

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: After advertising her home on Airbnb, Mandy takes in a guest from Russia - and they soon become more than friends. But can he be trusted? Comedy, written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan, with Tony Way (After Life)

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: Lola has not had a customer in her beauty salon for two days so Mandy suggests she introduce fish pedicures. Only to buy the wrong kind of fish. Sean Lock guest stars in the comedy, with Diane Morgan and Michelle Greenidge

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: A window cleaner, a children's party entertainer and a train supervisor compete to prove their profession is the toughest, with each spending a day working at the other's jobs

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Yana the tiger cracks a canine tooth and it must be removed, but it is not a simple procedure and the anaesthetic does not go to plan. Kate Humble and Ben Fogle present

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don gives tips on growing tomatoes and has ideas of how to fill gaps in the garden once the flowers of spring are over. Joe Swift visits the small city garden of an interior designer where some unusual plants have been used to add an element of surprise to the outdoor space, while in Kent Arit Anderson meets a community whose members have all been prescribed gardening as part of their recovery from physical and mental illness

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Lindy MacEwan thinks New Zealand could give her the fresh start she wants, but husband Steve has reservations, so they take their children down under for a trial week

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts the panel show that tests the wildlife knowledge of captains Chris Packham, Lucy Cooke and guests Steve Backshall and Ajay Tegala in a variety of rounds

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Douglas passes away suddenly after a party in the grounds of Ash Park, and Elizabeth asks George to contact James and get him to come home for the funeral

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter W, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Life in the Air
programme.desc: Wildlife series following animals that are able to defy gravity in various ways. The first programme features caracal cats, which jump to catch birds out of the air, and reveals how kangaroos' hopping movements help them find water in the desert. Leaping snakes are able to glide without wings in the jungles of Borneo while insects are able to accelerate up to 700G

programme.title: World's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham presents another selection of bizarre facts and occurrences, including a glacier that bleeds red, an electrician who got tattooed by a lightning bolt and the remarkable story of a man with a tooth for an eye

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Philip Serrell hunt for objects to take to auction at Blackburn Cathedral, Lancashire. Charles values a wooden Fijian club, while two vintage enamel signs catch Philip's eye. Presenter Paul Martin takes a look at the impressive Talbot collection - local press photographs taken of events, celebrities and the royal family over a 50-year period

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angellica Bell, Gyles Brandreth, Scarlett Moffatt and Iain Stirling test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: Gardening Together with Diarmuid Gavin
programme.desc: The presenter helps design a garden for the Montgomery family in Bainbridge, Co Down, who have a spectacular view but a lacklustre garden to see it from. He offers advice for gardeners with small spaces with some help from renowned head gardener Darragh Stone, and gives his mother's Dublin balcony a new lease of life

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Highlights of the fifth and final day of the Third Test, taking place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton. This is the final scheduled day of Test cricket in what has been a remarkable and unique summer, with both the West Indies and Pakistan helping England to fill their calendar as much as possible, when the possibility of a damaging financial wipeout loomed large

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: A Yorkshire hill farming family that keeps sheep and cows diversifies by investing in 300 goats, which are sold not just for meat and dairy products, but also goat milk soap. In the Lake District, Matt and Dani Blair settle into their new farm while also awaiting the birth of their first child - but a mix-up with the measuring tape proves costly and an escapee calf causes havoc. Crofter Graham is forced to adapt his working routine after a serious injury, and a storm threatens a Northumberland couple's plans to build a new home for their prize bull

programme.title: Manctopia: Billion Pound Property Boom
programme.desc: Local property developer Tim hits a million-pound problem in the mill he's converting into luxury apartments, while less than a mile away Anne and her friends face losing their homes to a giant redevelopment scheme. A homelessness support charity Lifeshare is forced to leave the Victorian Charter Street Mission, which it has operated out of for more than 30 years, due to redevelopment of the surrounding area. However, new businesses are also relocating to the city, which leads to two Glasgow IT workers looking for accommodation

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Eight Out of 10 Cats star Sean Lock hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of humour before introducing routines by grumpy stand-up Romesh Ranganathan and actor and funnyman Marcus Brigstocke

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Deforestation has skyrocketed in Colombia since the peace deal of 2016. Our World joins a team of scientists from the Royal Botanic Gardens in Kew as they go on a mission into virgin tropical rainforest. They hope to discover and save rare plant species before they are destroyed and vanish forever.

programme.title: The Unbelievable Story of Carl Beech
programme.desc: Vanessa Engle's documentary telling the story of the man who claimed he had been sexually abused by a group of prominent men in the 1970s and '80s, naming Jimmy Savile, ex-prime minister Edward Heath and former home secretary Leon Brittan as among those involved. The film is constructed using Beech's own words, taken from media and police interviews and from his blogs, and there are interviews with those who were close to the story at the time, including his ex-wife and Diana Clemetson, the widow of Lord Brittan

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: George gives a greenhouse update and shows the benefits of late sown cucumbers. In Carole's countryside garden, she deals with vine weevil, a persistent garden pest

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on further highlights from 15 years of the show, remembering the contestants' first experience of a professional kitchen, recalling those who thrived and those who only just survived the intense pressure. They also revisit a semi-final challenge from 2013 in which Janet Street-Porter, Shane Lynch, Les Dennis, Speech Debelle, Brian Capron and Adrian Edmondson cooked a three-course meal for 150 students, parents and fellows at Cambridge University's Gonville and Caius College

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Wass and Jeremy Dein return to investigate more historical murder cases, beginning with an infamous Victorian poisoning in which a woman was sentenced to death for killing her husband with arsenic

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: A plumber, a tour guide and a dog groomer compete to prove their profession is the toughest, with each having to spend a day working at the other's jobs

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble meets an ostrich that thinks it is a zebra, Ben Fogle takes a test to identify all the park's guinea pigs by name, and Jean Johansson watches lions climbing trees

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Monica Galetti and Giles Coren visit Schloss Elmau, a hotel located deep in the snowy Bavarian Alps that is promoted as providing a cultural hideaway that allows guests the chance to restore their mind, body and soul. Monica joins in one of the meditation sessions held every day for the restaurant staff, and Giles recites poetry and travels to a mountain hut to serve dinner, while dressed in lederhosen. They join the hotel guests for an evening performance featuring world-renowned musicians playing for free

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Fearons, from Dagenham in east London, sample life in Australia. Husband Sheldon feels such a permanent move will help his family enjoy a better pace of life, but wife Shelly Ann is struggling with leaving her life in the UK behind. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant has to use judgement and tactics to decide which other player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts the panel show that tests the wildlife knowledge of team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke, with guests Vim Kumaratunga and Lindsey Chapman

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: George is still depressed about James's rejection of life at Ash Park, while Sarah realises he is neglecting David. Elizabeth ignores an important note from Douglas's solicitor

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter I, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Life in the Air
programme.desc: The series turns to animals that fly, including footage of half a million mother bats, an agile sparrowhawk raiding for food, and large beetles taking to the sky as part of their mating ritual. The programme also reveals how peregrine falcons are able to reach speeds of 200mph, and why an albatross's nose helps it to fly. Narrated by Suranne Jones

programme.title: World's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham discovers the secrets behind a flame-filled firenado, unearths a strange-looking living rock and meets a team of oversized rats who save lives by sniffing out landmines

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Blackburn, Anita Manning values a gold charm bracelet and Philip Serrell takes a shine to an unusual globe. Paul Martin finds out about the life of singer Kathleen Ferrier

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angellica Bell, Gyles Brandreth, Scarlett Moffatt and Iain Stirling test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: Gardening Together with Diarmuid Gavin
programme.desc: The horticultural designer fashions a garden for the Hanway family in Howth, who have a shed-load of ideas for their patch, but no firm plan. For those wanting to live the good life themselves, Diarmuid gets advice from chef Rachel Allen, who helps him fulfil his dream of keeping chickens in the garden

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing 2016 champions Ore Oduba and Joanne Clifton swap the ballroom for the saleroom as they travel around Sussex and Kent in search of bargains, with the help of experts Catherine Southon and David Harper. Along the way, Ore learns how cricket has its origins in a game invented by milk maids 600 years ago, and Joanne finds out about vintage movie-making in Shoreham-by-Sea. But which of the duo will make the most profit when they go to auction in Southend?

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On a railway journey from the capital Palermo, through the ancient town of Agrigento and the port of Siracusa, to Europe's largest volcano, Mount Etna, Michael explores Sicilian life under the dictatorship. He also finds out how the dictator took on the Mafia and asks whether it is true that under Mussolini, the trains ran on time

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Period drama set in London in 1763 following a woman as she struggles to reconcile her roles as mother and brothel owner. The body of Sir George Howard is found and given the testimony of Thomas Haxby, the main suspects are Charlotte Wells and Daniel Marney, who were seen at his property and - according to him - were covered in mud and blood. In attempting to save one daughter, Margaret has seemingly condemned the other. Starring Hugh Skinner, Edward Hogg, Jessica Brown Findlay and Rory Fleck Bryne

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Daniel and Charlotte await trial for the murder of Sir George. Lydia tries to take down Margaret Wells once and for all, while Harriet unites with North in one final attempt to steal her children back from Benjamin Lennox. Starring Rory Fleck Byrne, Jessica Brown Findlay and Hugh Skinner

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland
programme.desc: Paul Murton goes on a caving expedition under the summit of Blaven

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Katie Razzall

programme.title: Manctopia: Billion Pound Property Boom
programme.desc: Local property developer Tim hits a million-pound problem in the mill he's converting into luxury apartments, while less than a mile away Anne and her friends face losing their homes to a giant redevelopment scheme. A homelessness support charity Lifeshare is forced to leave the Victorian Charter Street Mission, which it has operated out of for more than 30 years, due to redevelopment of the surrounding area. However, new businesses are also relocating to the city, which leads to two Glasgow IT workers looking for accommodation

programme.title: Inside the Bruderhof
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of a community of radical Christians living a remarkable back-to-basics life in a quiet British village. The 300 residents of Darvell in Sussex - one of 23 Bruderhof communities around the world - live outside mainstream society. They have relinquished money and possessions to live as disciples as Jesus, running an almost self-sufficient community. The film follows three of the residents, including inquisitive 18-year-old Hannah, who is starting to question her way of life. Before making a lifelong commitment, she journeys to London to find out how others live

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Three talented artists to make something special for the homes of people they've never met. In Lincolnshire, a buyer is looking for a gift for her husband as a thank-you for all he does for the family and to mark his long service to charity work. Will a stone sculptor, a mosaic maker or an illustrator create the perfect present? Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein re-examine the 1934 case of an off-duty soldier in financial difficulties who was convicted of murdering a friend on New Year's Eve

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Handmade in Africa
programme.desc: Seydou Kane crafts a new kora from scratch in his studio in downtown Dakar, Senegal, an instrument also known as a West African lute or harp that dates back to the 13th century

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene that includes luxurious green foliage and an old, weathered covered bridge placed lovingly among all the beauty

programme.title: Africa with Ade Adepitan
programme.desc: Ade explores central Africa, travelling through Gabon, the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda. He investigates plans to develop a sustainable version of palm oil farming, meets a champion boxer helping to rehabilitate child soldiers, and talks to one of Africa's most outspoken political campaigners. He also reports on how conflict between militia groups influenced by foreign powers lies at the heart of the problems that have made the Democratic Republic of Congo arguably the most chaotic and corrupt country in Africa, as well as discovering how the conflict has impacted on the country's few remaining gorillas

programme.title: African Renaissance: When Art Meets Power
programme.desc: Afua Hirsch explores the art, music and culture of Senegal, discovering a country with a cultural influence far beyond its size, with dynamic film, fashion and hip-hop scenes

programme.title: Africa's Great Civilisations
programme.desc: Henry Louis Gates Jr focuses on an era of great commercial and manufacturing growth throughout several regions on the continent. Beginning with the revolutionary transformation of North and West Africa, he travels to the shores of the Sahara Desert, where farmers, traders, warriors and nomads turned this region into the crossroads of some of history's most advanced, and wealthiest, civilizations

programme.title: Africa's Great Civilisations
programme.desc: Henry Louis Gates Jr looks at the powerful, cosmopolitan cities that dotted Africa at the time when Europe was in its Middle Ages. From 1000 to 1600, commerce, wealth and prosperity expanded across Africa, building new cities and founding new powerful states that mark this golden age

programme.title: Inside Cinema: Meet the Family
programme.desc: Part one of two. A look at family in cinema in all their maddening, dysfunctional glory, with clans in Aliens, Back to the Future, Home Alone, Tokyo Story, Pan's Labyrinth and Gosford Park among those analysed. Kathy Burke narrates

programme.title: Jigs & Wigs
programme.desc: Master tapper Chris Naish follows up on the success of his online exhibitions by challenging himself to put together a new dance crew to shoot another video within the space of a week

programme.title: Jigs & Wigs
programme.desc: The Zephaniah Dance Ministry comes together in Portadown to spread the word of the gospels through Irish dance, under the guidance of teacher Deborah Anderson

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a scene that includes luxurious green foliage and an old, weathered covered bridge placed lovingly among all the beauty

programme.title: African Renaissance: When Art Meets Power
programme.desc: Afua Hirsch explores the art, music and culture of Senegal, discovering a country with a cultural influence far beyond its size, with dynamic film, fashion and hip-hop scenes

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: The amateur cameraman's plans to film the area's wildlife in the snow are interrupted when a neighbour brings him an injured buzzard to tend. He also investigates what happened to one of the foals after the round-up

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a peaceful landscape, with distant foothills and velvety shrubs surrounding the quiet waters of an inlet

programme.title: Billy Connolly: A Scot in the Arctic
programme.desc: The comic heads for the North Pole, braving sub-zero temperatures and dealing with a persistent film crew to experience life in Canada's far North West. His only other company are the inquisitive polar bears who occasionally pay him a visit. First shown in 1995

programme.title: Wild Scotland
programme.desc: Gordon Buchanan takes a trip home to the island of Mull where he has just one day to film the rare sea eagle. While searching for a nest site, he spots a host of other wildlife

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: The second part of the documentary examines how promising lines in the Yorkshire Ripper inquiry were overlooked as a hoax tape took centre stage

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Capt Blackadder sees a chance to get some rest while hunting for a spy in the field hospital, and decides he is not going to let his holiday be cut short just because he has found an obvious suspect with a German accent. Comedy, guest starring Miranda Richardson, with Rowan Atkinson and Tony Robinson

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Capt Blackadder tries every trick in the book to escape the trenches before the big push, including pretending to be insane - but ends up going over the top with sidekicks Baldrick and George. Final episode of the comedy, guest starring Geoffrey Palmer, with Rowan Atkinson, Tony Robinson and Hugh Laurie

programme.title: Talking Comedy
programme.desc: A look back through the archives at Rowan Atkinson's appearances on some of the BBC's best-loved talk shows

programme.title: Art on the BBC
programme.desc: Documentary using clips from the BBC archives to explore how six decades of TV has influenced the understanding of the arts. In the first edition, Kate Bryan looks at the use of the nude by both artists and film-makers as a means to shock their audiences and challenge conventions

programme.title: How to Get Ahead
programme.desc: The three-part documentary continues as writer and broadcaster Stephen Smith explores Renaissance Florence under the reign of Grand Duke Cosimo Medici, whose fledgling court prized the finer things in life. Successful courtiers had to have brains as well as brawn, and some looked to theorists like Niccolo Machiavelli for underhand ways to get ahead, while enlightened polymaths turned their minds to the heavens - and to ice-cream

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a peaceful landscape, with distant foothills and velvety shrubs surrounding the quiet waters of an inlet

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: The amateur cameraman's plans to film the area's wildlife in the snow are interrupted when a neighbour brings him an injured buzzard to tend. He also investigates what happened to one of the foals after the round-up

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: The second part of the documentary examines how promising lines in the Yorkshire Ripper inquiry were overlooked as a hoax tape took centre stage

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: A quiet January for the amateur cameraman soon turns busy when wild boar become loose in the Devonshire countryside, bringing Britain's media to the area. Johnny also films spoonbills, finding a unique way to get close to the migrating birds

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a night scene, with waves crashing against rocks and a lighthouse

programme.title: Monty Don's Japanese Gardens
programme.desc: Part two of two. The horticultural enthusiast explores Japan during autumn. He begins as he did in the spring, at one of the 'three great gardens of Japan' to learn how they are protected from the coming winter. Monty follows the history of the Japanese garden through the military strongholds of past leaders to the many styles, new and old, of the iconic stroll garden and finds a slice of Victorian England in the heart of Tokyo. He also learns how the Japanese are weaving nature into their concrete urban jungle. He ends his journey by the Sea of Japan at a place that makes art out of its gardens

programme.title: Japan: Earth's Enchanted Islands
programme.desc: In the south-west of Japan, the Ryukyu Islands stretch towards the tropics and all life on them is influenced by the power of the sea, with volcanos and typhoons forces to be reckoned with. Michelle Dockery narrates a journey beginning at an island at the top of the chain and travelling south, revealing unexpected stories of isolation, unique wildlife and unsolved mysteries

programme.title: The Art of Japanese Life
programme.desc: James Fox explores how the art and culture produced by cities past and present has shaped the country's attitudes and helped forge the very idea of Japan itself

programme.title: The Art of Japanese Life
programme.desc: James Fox explores the art of the Japanese home, the clean minimalism of which has been exported round the world from modernist architecture to lifestyle stores

programme.title: People's Palaces: The Golden Age of Civic Architecture
programme.desc: Part one of two. Architectural historian Dr Jonathan Foyle explores some of the most impressive Georgian and Victorian buildings in the north of England, beginning with neo-classical constructions. He visits town and concert halls, libraries, schools and galleries in Liverpool, Leeds, Bradford and Manchester to find out how the regular proportion and symmetry of the style was intended to suggest order in chaotically expanding urban environments

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a night scene, with waves crashing against rocks and a lighthouse

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor

programme.title: The Beauty of Maps
programme.desc: Cartography through the ages, beginning with the Hereford Mappa Mundi, the largest intact medieval wall map in the world, which was intended to picture all of human knowledge in a single image. The programme traces its history, and features interviews with people who have been affected by it, including Turner Prize-winning artist Grayson Perry

programme.title: Japan: Earth's Enchanted Islands

The fisherman wakes to discover a long, long, long fish at the end of his line. The Twirlywoo Screen draws a small line that Chickedy and Chick help make longer. Toodloo helps the line get a little longer still but when Great BigHoo taps the screen, the line becomes so long it shoots off the Twirlywoo Screen. programme.desc: The young lion agrees to do too many things with people, but discovers he may not be able to complete them all

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Singer Yabba loses her voice just before a talent show, leaving Timmy to save the day by performing instead

programme.title: Bing

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos open and close the curtains on a stage, while a very important singer is trying to perform

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A boy learns to knit with purple wool and in Teletubbyland, purple Tinky Winky answers the Tubby Phone and leads the Tubby Phone Dance

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Babies and their grown-ups play with tubes and enjoy songs and story time together

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: On a lovely day, the squirrels play the Shape game outside. However, they find working out which shape goes where is quite tricky

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: Sid Sloane steps through the Magic Curtain to become a painter and decorator, and is employed to help a team transform one of the biggest rooms in Lord Poshington's huge home

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: When Mum leaves a can of air freshener on the counter, it's up to Pablo and his friends to stop The Smell Gobbler eating all the smells in the house - including the nice ones

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Twins Daisy and Lily take a trip to the beach with their cousins. Dogs Foxy and Saffy join in too, and love chasing after balls on the sand. After all the seaside fun, the whole family heads to the beach cafe to enjoy an ice-cream. Back at home, the sisters take care of a special visitor - Grandma Owl the school cuddly toy. Together they embark on an adventure around the house and take lots of photos

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: The story of how the tortoise's shell became bumpy and broken

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Children from all over the world demonstrate their favourite recipes, and find out where the ingredients they have used come from

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: The Brooklyn-Bells decorate Doug's bedroom. Waffle tries to help but causes a green paint disaster and escapes into Mrs Hobbs' house next door

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The friends play with a ball, only to find Timmy keeps hogging it

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Mrs J is happy with the Wobbly Coffe Pot Cafe makeover, but her customers are less keen

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The pair help Tortoise find a quiet place to sleep when it is time for him to hibernate

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: The animated adventures of an energetic baby monkey and his long-suffering mother, who take a light-hearted approach to problem-solving and teamwork as they tackle life's challenges together

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Drummer D has his drumsticks but nothing to play on, so his friends help him find something he can use to deliver a dazzling solo

programme.title: Yakka Dee!

programme.title: Numberblocks

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The friends play with a dressing up box, but have an argument over who gets to wear the only crown

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos watch a man practising his tennis strokes using a machine to shoot the balls to him, which prompts them to learn more about speed

programme.title: Something Special

programme.title: Teletubbies

programme.title: Tinpo

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: It's Gurdy's lots-of-birthdays-birthday

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: Pablo thinks he'll float away if he puts down his pine cones. Draff disagrees, but when Pablo puts them down, he floats up into the sky and his friends have to rescue him

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo dreams about flying with dragons and racing with cheetahs even when he is awake, leaving him questioning whether the fantasies are real or imagined

programme.title: Time for School

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Gem and her teams of Swashbucklers try to win her jewels back from pirates Cook, Line and Sinker. Game show for children

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is set to make the squirrels juice from fresh fruit in the garden, but while picking the fruit the Squirrels meet a little bug, full of woes about being too small. The Squirrels think it must be brilliant to be so tiny but the little bug disagrees

programme.desc: Flopsy and Mopsy insist on accompanying Peter on one of his adventures, and he discovers there may be more to his twin sisters than he first thought

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie examines food and drink, using a slow-motion camera to find out how bubbles get inside a bottle of fizzy water, and visiting a dairy to see how milk is collected from cows
