programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Nazanin Zaghari-Ratcliffe has been held prisoner in Iran for more than four years after being convicted of spying charges. Nobody knows how many other British citizens have been imprisoned by the Iranian authorities, because the UK government refuses to say. Reporter Darragh MacIntyre meets the families of some of those who have been arbitrarily detained and asks whether the payment of a historic debt could set them free.

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Linda Henry and Luisa Bradshaw-White, aka sisters Shirley and Tina Carter, who talk about their characters' most memorable moments and revealing some of their best behind-the-scenes stories. Linda chats about how fans react to seeing 'Shirley' in the street, Luisa recalls her own brush with musical superstardom, and the pair explain how a tender scene quickly became a riot of laughter and chaos every time the cameras cut

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: A Friday evening trip out in the car ends in a fatal crash, but the driver refuses to accept his driving is to blame. The collision team must find the evidence to prove the real story of the night

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: Maan's disgrace has a disastrous effect on the Kapoor family, but when the bad press is used against his father in the general election campaign, it results in an unexpected gesture from an old friend. Following the revelation of further interference from her meddling family and emboldened by casting her vote in India's first national democratic election, Lata makes a decision about her future. Concluding part of Andrew Davies' adaptation of Vikram Seth's novel, starring Tanya Maniktala

programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Lorraine Stanley, Jessica Plummer and Toby-Alexander Smith to relive some of their characters' most iconic moments, share stories and reveal stuff that is normally kept under wraps

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Part one of two. Michael Mosley and Mehreen Baig investigate the lack of regulation in the cosmetic surgery industry. The programme examines how potentially risky surgeries have been replaced by non-invasive procedures like injectable fillers and fat freezing, and puts the science and safety of these new approaches to the test. The first episode focuses on facial procedures

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder follows the story of his grandfather who survived the Holocaust, hearing first-hand testimony of the horror of Nazi forced-labour camps as well as of the hope offered by a new life in Lake Windermere. Investigating the dark mystery surrounding his great-grandfather leads Robert to a small town in Latvia, where he uncovers a story of mental illness and trauma that will ultimately, he hopes, lay some ghosts to rest.

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce looks for a way to better cope with her anxiety, attending a yoga class with a difference and discovering the benefits of performing random acts of kindness to strangers. She also seeks the advice of actress-turned-blogger Holly Matthews, who has learned to find peace following a terrible tragedy in her life

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Reflecting on the changing state of the nation as lockdown draws to an end, Michelle is joined by guest RuPaul, The Vivienne and Rylan Clark-Neal, organises a surprise Strictly Come Dancing reunion, and has an unexpected visit from eldest daughter Lillie

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic editions of the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this episode, the contestants include a pest controller, a slam poet and a train signaller

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: The park sees the formation of its first ever all female wolf pack, and Ben gets a peek at some incredible footage of male koala, Burke

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a property in Birmingham with some mesmerising wallpaper and a south Wales house that has her dancing with joy. Meanwhile, Dion Dublin is in Liverpool to see a place that has him a bit confused

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: An oil rig worker from Tyne and Wear is rushed to the A&E department of Gilbert Bain Hospital with a suspected stroke

programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp oversees proceedings as Philip Serrell and Catherine Southon help two teams scour Exeter's Westpoint Arena for items to take to auction in Ottery St Mary

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Hamid demands Kam come clean at school, and the proposed future for the Towers riles the residents. Starring Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke

programme.desc: A team of TV news presenters takes on general knowledge champions the Goliaths, as Babita Sharma, Charlie Stayt, Sally Magnusson, Martine Croxall and Clive Myrie set out to secure a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton goes house hunting with a couple who want their £950,000 budget to buy them a family home in the West Sussex countryside that will give them a better work-life balance. While there, the presenter heads to the seaside town of Worthing, and finds out how one of the oldest working cinemas in the country has been saved from redevelopment

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Tree surgeon Olly, sandwich factory supervisor Ruth and zookeeper Charlotte compete, taking turns at doing one another's jobs before one of them walks away with the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell head to an auction in Hamilton. Steph comes across a chic French sign, but Phil is distracted by the local cuisine

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Following the life of 57-year-old Lorraine, a tribute act to the American pop star, Pink, exploring what it takes to spend your life pretending to be someone else

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a rusty toy pedal car that is missing its steering wheel, a Victorian stamp perforating machine, a bronze statue of a dagger-wielding assassin by leading French artist Claire Colinet, and a baize-covered card table dating from the 1940s that has sentimental value for its owner

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: When his father misses an appointment with his mental health worker, 25-year-old Oli calls Gloucestershire Police, concerned about the whereabouts of his father, Michael, who suffers from paranoid schizophrenia and is thought to have stopped taking his medication. With no sign of Michael overnight, police launch an appeal for information, and CCTV searches of the town reveal that Michael got into a taxi two nights before and was taken to a high point in the Cotswold's. Officers prepare for a major search operation, but looking for someone hiding in a heavily wooded area presents them with major challenges

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: Supernatural drama set in an alternate America, following three young witches from basic training in combat magic into early deployment on the military frontline to fight against a dangerous rebel group called The Spree. Starring Taylor Hickson, Jessica Sutton and Ashley Nicole Williams

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: The unit travels to Salem Town for an annual witch celebration, but an unexpected disturbance throws the proceedings into chaos. Meanwhile, Tally wonders if she made the right choice to embark on a military career. Supernatural drama, starring Taylor Hickson and Jessica Sutton

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on further highlights from 15 years of the show, remembering some of the most entertaining team challenges - and how different dynamics can lead to either success or chaos in the kitchen. They also revisit a 2015 semi-final task which saw Sheree Murphy, Scott Maslen, Rylan Clark-Neal, Sam Nixon, Chesney Hawkes, Mica Paris, Kimberly Wyatt and Tom Parker thrown together in a Victorian-style village, using only ingredients widely available during that era

programme.title: Disclosure
programme.desc: The global pandemic has changed everyone's lives and will continue to do so for years to come. People with disabilities have faced big challenges - from loss of essential services and support, to abuse and a lack of understanding of their needs in public spaces. Ian Hamilton investigates what life under lockdown has meant for people with disabilities and asks whether their human rights are in danger as everyday life changes to deal with coronavirus

programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder follows the story of his grandfather who survived the Holocaust, hearing first-hand testimony of the horror of Nazi forced-labour camps as well as of the hope offered by a new life in Lake Windermere. Investigating the dark mystery surrounding his great-grandfather leads Robert to a small town in Latvia, where he uncovers a story of mental illness and trauma that will ultimately, he hopes, lay some ghosts to rest.

programme.title: How's Your Head, Hun?
programme.desc: Reflecting on the changing state of the nation as lockdown draws to an end, Michelle is joined by guest RuPaul, The Vivienne and Rylan Clark-Neal, organises a surprise Strictly Come Dancing reunion, and has an unexpected visit from eldest daughter Lillie

programme.title: Hayley Goes...

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover

programme.title: Blood of the Clans
programme.desc: When Bonnie Prince Charlie arrives in Scotland to regain the crown of his ancestors, the clans are called upon to rise in armed revolt for him. Many do, and they invade England, but others oppose him and join the British side to fight against the rebels. Some though play a cunning double game, pretending to be loyal to the British whilst secretly raising clansmen for the Prince. Presented by Neil Oliver

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: When his father misses an appointment with his mental health worker, 25-year-old Oli calls Gloucestershire Police, concerned about the whereabouts of his father, Michael, who suffers from paranoid schizophrenia and is thought to have stopped taking his medication. With no sign of Michael overnight, police launch an appeal for information, and CCTV searches of the town reveal that Michael got into a taxi two nights before and was taken to a high point in the Cotswold's. Officers prepare for a major search operation, but looking for someone hiding in a heavily wooded area presents them with major challenges

programme.title: Gardening Together with Diarmuid Gavin
programme.desc: Diarmuid helps Rachel Kelly in Drumbo, Co Down, who wants to get rid of an eyesore in her garden. He is joined by plant experts Paul Smyth and Colm O'Driscoll to offer advice ,and reveals whether a huge greenhouse made of reclaimed materials has made the deadline in his own garden

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Linda Henry and Luisa Bradshaw-White, aka sisters Shirley and Tina Carter, who talk about their characters' most memorable moments and revealing some of their best behind-the-scenes stories. Linda chats about how fans react to seeing 'Shirley' in the street, Luisa recalls her own brush with musical superstardom, and the pair explain how a tender scene quickly became a riot of laughter and chaos every time the cameras cut

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Nazanin Zaghari-Ratcliffe has been held prisoner in Iran for more than four years after being convicted of spying charges. Nobody knows how many other British citizens have been imprisoned by the Iranian authorities, because the UK government refuses to say. Reporter Darragh MacIntyre meets the families of some of those who have been arbitrarily detained and asks whether the payment of a historic debt could set them free.

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: Maan's disgrace has a disastrous effect on the Kapoor family, but when the bad press is used against his father in the general election campaign, it results in an unexpected gesture from an old friend. Following the revelation of further interference from her meddling family and emboldened by casting her vote in India's first national democratic election, Lata makes a decision about her future. Concluding part of Andrew Davies' adaptation of Vikram Seth's novel, starring Tanya Maniktala

programme.title: Inside Autism: A Tiny Bit of Magic
programme.desc: Documentary following children with autism spending a week at an intensive therapy camp where specialist therapists try to transform challenging behaviours in just seven days. Eight-year-old Abel puts everything into his mouth and his parents are terrified that one day he is going to choke or poison himself, while seven-year-old Leo won't shower or brush his teeth and fights off any attempt to cut his hair

programme.title: Famalam

programme.desc: The coronavirus pandemic has presented humanity with an almighty shock. Our evermore interconnected and technologically advanced societies are now in lockdown and we are fearful for our health and economic futures thanks to an invisible virus. Stephen Sackur speaks to the Israeli historian and best-selling author Yuval Noah Harari. What 21st century lesson can we draw from the spread of Covid-19? First shown on the BBC News Channel on Tuesday 28 April.

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Belle shocks the Dingles, Vinny hits a nerve and Tracy puts pressure on Nate

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Peter gets in the way of Abi's morphine fix, and, desperately worried about Todd, Sean searches various social media sites hoping for clues - but is left frustrated. Nicky puts Daniel in touch with another escort

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The naked bodies of three men are found placed in the shape of a triangle, leading Jack to suspect the work of a satanic cult. Meanwhile, press baron James Callum's plans to make his mark on Denton are hindered by an investigation into his lover's death in a houseboat fire - and the suspicious demise of his former girlfriend may be the key to unmasking the killer. Starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Adrian Lukis, Sarah Matravers and Paul Shane

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: In 2019, the airport is having its busiest year to date, and are expecting more than 81 million passengers to fly in and out of the terminals, with its 76,000 staff ready to take on whatever is thrown at them. Officers Terry and Dave deal with an unruly passenger threatening to close the airport, while ramp team member Rachel is dishing out safety fines, and in Terminal 2 Security, Estefania is speechless with someone's carry-on luggage

programme.title: When English Football Ruled Europe
programme.desc: A look at English clubs' dominance of the European Cup between 1977 and 1984. The second programme looks at the breakup of Brian Clough's hugely successful Nottingham Forest, and the re-emergence of Liverpool, who lifted their third and fourth European cups in 1981 and 1984. Plus, the incredible story of Aston Villa, who upset all the odds to claim their one and only win in the competition in 1982

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley.

programme.title: Parveen's Indian Kitchen
programme.desc: The food writer prepares a vegan recipe for tarka daal, provides a simple guide to making chappatis, and cooks her son's favourite meal - keema aloo matter with basmati rice. In Amritsar, she visits one of the biggest kitchens in the world, while chef Gayatri Peshwaria teaches children to cook halwa, while in London she meets the owners of a London restaurant bringing a taste of Calcutta to the capital

programme.desc: Text-based information service

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Shaun, Sam, Jackie and Gayle pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa and Martin have their first therapy session together - though neither of them was expecting they would be given homework - while Morwenna is not happy about it when a teenager on work experience starts trying to boss her around in the surgery. Mrs Tishell is surprised when her husband Clive returns and tells her he wants to give things another go, and Louisa has to call Martin when one of her pupils collapses during a school excursion. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Another chance to see John Thaw's farewell appearance as Colin Dexter's Oxford detective. Back from sick leave, Morse uncovers new evidence concerning the year-long investigation into the murder of Yvonne Harrison, but has to cope with playing second fiddle to long-standing sidekick Lewis - and there are questions about whether he is fit enough to handle the case. Kevin Whately co-stars, with James Grout, Jesse Birdsall, Clare Holman and Paul Freeman

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall
programme.desc: Observational documentary going behind the scenes of Prince Charles's private estate the Duchy of Cornwall, which he has built up over the past 50 years into a business to support himself, his immediate family and his charities. The first of two parts reveals how the Duchy is run to reflect the prince's personal philosophies, and also explores how the estate supports an ancient way of life on Bodmin Moor but also gives young families the opportunity to embark on a new start in farming

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five friends from Hertfordshire calling themselves Herts & Minds join forces to tackle the quiz, hosted by Warwick Davis. They answer questions about top 10 lists, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his dealers are in Colwyn Bay in Wales, where James Layte is thrilled to stumble upon a Lewis Carroll first edition, Helen Gardiner falls for an oil lamp, and Mark Stevens comes across a car mascot

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five family members from Harrow, north-west London, answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Ailsa, Sarah, Kelly and Matt pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Marlon and Bob interfere, Nate reassures Tracy, while Paul cooks things up - but will it work?

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Carla is suspicious when Peter lies to her about going to meet Abi, and when she finds out he is helping her with her addiction, she warns him to be careful. When Gemma presents Paul with a signed photo of PJ and Duncan, Paul can hardly believe his eyes, while Jenny greets Johnny's return with hostility

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Airside officer Ian has a close shave out on the runway. Meanwhile, veteran Border Force officers Glen and Amarjit hunt down smuggled cargo

programme.title: Coronation Street Icons
programme.desc: Sally Lindsay celebrates the best moments from Sally Dynevor's 34-year career on Coronation Street, with the help of clips from the archives and interviews with co-stars. The actress reveals how she nearly messed up her role as Sally on her first day in 1986 because she dressed as a punk. Michael Le Vell recalls the moment he first set eyes on her, while other contributors include Brooke Vincent (Sophie Webster), Helen Flanagan (Rosie Webster), Joe Duttine (Tim Metcalfe), William Roache (Ken Barlow), David Neilson (Roy Cropper) and Beverley Callard (Liz McDonald)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective looks forward to a quiet Christmas, but is disturbed by the Foreign Office when an Egyptian prince loses a famous ruby - after making the mistake of lending it to a mysterious woman. Guest starring Stephanie Cole, Nigel Le Vaillant and Tariq Alibai

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: The Lancashire cricketer reflects on his life and career. He talks candidly about resorting to extreme measures as he battled with his weight, looks back at the key role he played in England's 2005 Ashes triumph and relives the victory celebrations - when he was asked to leave the Cabinet Room at 10 Downing Street for pretending to be the Prime Minister. 'Freddie' also discusses his recent return to the sport, after doctors advised him never to run again upon his retirement in 2010

programme.title: British Touring Car Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Steve Rider looks back at races seven, eight and nine of the British Touring Car Championship season at Oulton Park. Commentary comes from David Addison and Tim Harvey, with Louise Goodman reporting

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: James enters the Los Angeles in style while driving a vintage Porsche. After a visit to an incredible food market in Santa Monica, he heads to the beach to cook up a storm. He also meets a pensioner who happens to be a fitness coach, drops in on old friend and chef-to-the-stars Wolfgang Puck at his Beverly Hills restaurant CUT and finishes his visit with a bite of his favourite pizza

programme.desc: Robert Rinder hears the case of a man claiming £328 for two golf clubs that he lent to a friend and were subsequently stolen

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: The Cloud 9 employees and customers find themselves snowed in, leading to escalating tensions and infighting. Comedy, starring America Ferrera

programme.title: Dinner Date
programme.desc: Coren chooses three dates from five menus. The three men behind her chosen meals will each get the chance to entertain her, but she can choose only one chap to join her on another date. Which lucky fellow will join her for seconds?

programme.desc: In Cardiff, bricklayer Alex, support worker Kevin and plasterer Zhane are all hoping to win a date with Shannon, a self-proclaimed modern-day Marilyn Monroe

programme.desc: Harry Hill reveals how the craze of 'face change' is rendering photo identification useless, marvels at a performance by One Direction lookalikes and presents one of the most horrific videos seen on the show. programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps in a show that reveals what former PM David Cameron got up to behind the bins at Chequers. Plus, the host reveals the reason why Lorraine Kelly is testing Ross King's thongs

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill identifies more celebrity lookalikes in a comical selection of viewer-submitted videos, featuring doppelgangers of Matthew Kelly, Steven Spielberg and Osama bin Laden, plus the rare and moving sight of a car giving birth

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden's commitment is tested when Zoey and her daughter go to live with him. Meanwhile, Alan gets more than he bargained for when he moves in with Lyndsey. Comedy, starring Ashton Kutcher

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan suffers a minor heart attack and is admitted to hospital to recuperate. However, his time on the wards proves less than relaxing when he is visited by the ghost of his brother Charlie - who takes a wholly unexpected form. Comedy, guest starring Kathy Bates

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter discovers that he has a vestigial twin growing out of his neck and names him Chip. However, he soon becomes jealous of the attention the new arrival is receiving and decides to try to amputate his brother. Animated comedy, with the voice of Seth MacFarlane

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.title: Family Guy

programme.desc: Tony and Maxine spend the night together. Tricia thinks Sally is spying on her for Mike. Kevin is worried that the garage is going to go under and he will lose his job

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet, Lysette Anthony and Edward Fox

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Liz Merrick is mortified when one of her patients dies of an insulin overdose, but even more so when Sgt Merton is forced to investigate her responsibility. programme.title: Judi Dench's Wild Borneo Adventure
programme.desc: Part one of two. The award-winning actress embarks on a trip of a lifetime to uncover the lush rainforests, magical wildlife and spectacular coastlines of Malaysian Borneo. Judi experiences the rainforest for the first time in a thrilling 100-mile helicopter ride to the heart of the island with expert Glen Reynolds, before observing orangutans in the wild and learning how they are helping to prevent global warming

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: A look back at TV comedy from 1979. It was a pivotal year for Britain, with a new prime minister in Margaret Thatcher and mass strikes leading to the so-called 'Winter of Discontent'. Television saw a return to political satire, with the BBC series Not the Nine O'Clock News, though the northern club scene was dominating comedic output at the time with the likes of The Cannon & Ball Show and the stand-up series The Comedians. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin are mystified by the murders of a popular couple who were stabbed to death while they slept. Lacking any leads to possible grievances held or enemies made, the detectives suspect a case of mistaken identity, and the previous owner of the house - a banker caught up in a hedge-fund scandal - emerges as the more likely target. Guest starring Samuel West

programme.title: Prime Suspect 1973
programme.desc: Crime drama prequel following the early career of Jane Tennison, as she starts out as a single-minded 22-year-old probationary officer in 1970s London, where chauvinism and rule-bending are the norm. Jane is thrown into a brutal murder inquiry when the body of a teenage girl, Julie-Ann Collins, is found in the underground car park of a Hackney housing estate, while the family of local crime matriarch Renee Bentley are shaken by a violent attack on her. Stefanie Martini steps into the shoes of Helen Mirren to bring the character back to the small screen, with Sam Reid, Blake Harrison and Alun Armstrong also starring

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Ghosts from the past return to haunt Morse as he investigates the apparent suicide of a terminally ill Oxford don whose widow turns out to be the woman the detective was set to marry in his youth. As the case develops, Lewis worries his partner's professionalism is being undermined. Starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Joanna David, Samantha Bond, David Haig, Adrian Dunbar and James Grout

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: On doctor's orders, the dapper Belgian sleuth visits Brighton for what he hopes will be a relaxing holiday, but it leads to one of the most absorbing cases he has ever faced - the disappearance of a cache of jewels. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack

programme.desc: The receivers take over the garage and go through the books with Don. Kevin is made redundant with two weeks' pay. Maxine confesses to Fiona that she is going out with Tony

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth finds himself in interesting company as English socialites and American millionaires rub shoulders on board a train bound for the French Riviera. Passengers include oil magnate Rufus Van Aldin (Elliott Gould) and his daughter Ruth (Jaime Murray) - to whom he has given a magnificent ruby known as the Heart of Fire. With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve comes to the aid of an amnesiac struggling to piece together his identity, Jenny tells Dennis she is tired of playing second fiddle to his career, and Bernie and David step in when it appears Vernon's money-making schemes are getting the better of him. Rural drama, starring James Carlton, Ian East, Peter Benson, David Lonsdale and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper sleuth returns to his home country of Belgium for the first time since the First World War to attend a medal ceremony, only to find a murder case needs solving. Agatha Christie mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Chancellor and David De Keyser

programme.title: Judi Dench's Wild Borneo Adventure
programme.desc: The actress journeys more than 560km down Borneo's Kinabatangan river and along the island's coastline to explore the unique wildlife that lives in this threatened environment. With scientist Kerisha Kntayya, Judi joins a crocodile hunt with a difference. Kerisha plucks young crocodiles out of the water. Judi then joins Kerisha's team as they wrestle an adult croc as part of the study Kerisha hopes will help save these prehistoric creatures. The Oscar winner, who's had a fascination with bats from an early age, also explores the Gomantong cave, home to more than a million bats

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: Comedy milestones of 1984, when cult hits like The Young Ones and Alas Smith and Jones battled it out in the schedules with more mainstream fare like Russ Abbot's Madhouse, and politicians were targeted by one of the most irreverent TV shows of all time - Spitting Image. Fans also lost two of the greatest comedy stars of all time - Tommy Cooper and Eric Morecambe. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The team struggles to understand the involvement of 12-year-old Kaden Blake in the shooting of mother and carer Lia Brown. As a victim of gang culture, the boy's only protection is keeping silent, and by speaking out about the crime, he sets in motion a devastating chain of events. Guest starring Lucy Speed (EastEnders, The Bill), with Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Prime Suspect 1973
programme.desc: The police delve deeper into the murder of Julie-Ann Collins, setting their sights on the driver of the car the victim was last seen climbing into. However, as the hunt gets under way, relations become strained between Jane and DI Bradfield when she walks her drunken boss home one night. Meanwhile, Clifford Bentley is fuming over the attack on his wife by gangland kingpin Clay Whiteley and launches a revenge attack - putting his family's criminal interests in jeopardy. Stefanie Martini stars as the novice WPC who would eventually become the tough senior detective made famous by Helen Mirren, with Sam Reid and Alun Armstrong

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a well-known business tycoon leads Morse to investigate the man's feuding family, and inadvertently makes him the unwitting target of a hate campaign by the tabloids - a problem compounded by his uneasy relationship with a new chief superintendent. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star alongside Gwen Taylor, Martin Clunes, Alun Armstrong, Anna Massey, Charlotte Coleman and Rupert Graves

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Alf Ventress mounts a large-scale surveillance operation in an effort to track down the wool thief responsible for de-fleecing sheep in Aidensfield. Bernie finds himself in hot water when an estranged son arrives to bury his father - who is already six feet under. Drama, starring James Carlton and Peter Benson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Alison from Hertfordshire searches the sister she did not know existed for over 50 years, after discovering old family photographs in a wallet. The photographs showed Alison's mother holding a baby with 'Amanda' written on the back and a date of 1951, meaning Alison has a half-sister she never knew. Elsewhere in the programme, John, a builder from Yorkshire, is haunted by not knowing his father, and after applying for his adoption files, he begins the search for his birth parents, Marlene and Kenneth

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: Important comedy milestones of 1998, including The Royle Family and Goodness Gracious Me, and the final episodes of Drop the Dead Donkey and Father Ted. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: DS Sam Casey, a young officer with a strong sense of justice, is brought in to investigate the shooting of Devlin outside the Old Bailey, and is surprised to learn the gunman was specifically targeting police officers. The case sees the whole team emotionally spent, with the trial itself at risk of being derailed by a politically driven defence lawyer (Colin Salmon) with an axe to grind. Crime drama, starring Bradley Walsh, Freema Agyeman and Paul Nicholls, who joins the cast

programme.title: Prime Suspect 1973
programme.desc: Following the revelation of the red Jaguar in George and Mary Collins' garage, the pair protest their innocence about their daughter's final days - but the case against them hardens when forensics officers discover further signs of violence against Julie Ann in their home. With the timeline of Julie Ann's injuries not adding up, Jane pursues a witness who say Eddie visited George Collins the night before her death, while Clifford Bentley is released from prison and returns home to a hero's welcome

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Leaving behind his beloved Oxford, Morse and a reluctant Lewis set off to investigate the bizarre death of an Englishwoman who was attending a psychotherapy clinic in northern Italy. Their inquiries lead them to the vineyards and villas of Vicenza and the city of Verona - as well as a glamorous opera singer and a convicted fraudster. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star as the detective duo, with Frances Barber, Michael Kitchen, James Grout and Jolyon Baker

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper sleuth returns to his home country of Belgium for the first time since the First World War to attend a medal ceremony, only to find a murder case needs solving. Agatha Christie mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Chancellor and David De Keyser

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie reveals everything that's happened between him and Belle to Kim over the phone. When Nate arrives, ready to plead for his job, they're left wondering how much he overheard. Elsewhere, Vinny tells his father that they'll be able to subtly convince Mandy to get back together with him

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie offers Nate his job back, plus a bonus if he agrees to keep the secret. Dan is panicking as he tries to keep his financial troubles a secret from Amelia, and Leyla offers Gabby an apprenticeship at Take a Vow

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally asks Mike for a job as a trainee machinist. The Council agrees to rename Coronation Street after Alf. Steve is pleased to hear Fiona and Tony have split

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The day of Kim's funeral arrives. The Dingles kidnap one of the thugs and hold him hostage. Biff and Linda are shocked to find an abandoned baby on their doorstep

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff and Linda take the abandoned baby to the police. DI Cooke questions Zoe about the cufflink. Sophie clashes with Chris after he gets drunk

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Crane and Merton have their hands full when a sniper strikes during a clay-pigeon shoot, and Bellamy's fiery temper only serves to make matters worse. Elsewhere, Vernon decides to venture into the B&B market - but his cooking skills leave a lot to be desired. Rural drama, starring James Carlton, Duncan Bell, Mark Jordon and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean spies on Sid when his bad timekeeping makes her suspicious of his activities. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean is delighted when Sid actually agrees with her for once, but their plans for the day end in disaster. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: As the Ropers settle into their new home, George realises he is going to have to look for a job. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Liz Merrick is mortified when one of her patients dies of an insulin overdose, but even more so when Sgt Merton is forced to investigate her responsibility. Meanwhile, Vernon Scripps embarks on a mission to make Aidensfield sparkle with his own brand of cleaning products. Drama, starring Aislin McGuckin and Duncan Bell

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank cannot believe that Zoe doubts his innocence. Kim's solicitor arrives for the reading of her will which throws up some surprises for Zoe and Chris

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Glovers are stunned to hear that Emma is the mother of the abandoned baby. Zoe finds Chris feeling sorry for himself. The Windsors arrive home to find Kelly has left with Tom

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: In an attempt to stop Sally working, Kevin tells her he will buy into the garage. Alf plans a party for the residents to announce the renaming. Maxine makes a pass at Tony

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tony and Maxine spend the night together. Tricia thinks Sally is spying on her for Mike. Kevin is worried that the garage is going to go under and he will lose his job

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A philandering husband is found dying by the swimming pool after being shot during a house party at the home of Lady Lucy Angkatell. Legendary Belgian Sleuth Poirot, who is guest of honour at the gathering, appears to have an open-and-shut case on his hands - but the investigator finds himself up against one of his finest adversaries. Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet, Lysette Anthony and Edward Fox

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Liz Merrick is mortified when one of her patients dies of an insulin overdose, but even more so when Sgt Merton is forced to investigate her responsibility. Meanwhile, Vernon Scripps embarks on a mission to make Aidensfield sparkle with his own brand of cleaning products. Drama, starring Aislin McGuckin and Duncan Bell

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings and the Belgian supersleuth investigate a baffling gangland murder which draws them both into the underworld of London's Little Italy. Miss Lemon turns out to have a link with the victim's valet. Whodunit, starring David Suchet and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Judi Dench's Wild Borneo Adventure
programme.desc: Part one of two. The award-winning actress embarks on a trip of a lifetime to uncover the lush rainforests, magical wildlife and spectacular coastlines of Malaysian Borneo. Judi experiences the rainforest for the first time in a thrilling 100-mile helicopter ride to the heart of the island with expert Glen Reynolds, before observing orangutans in the wild and learning how they are helping to prevent global warming

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: A look back at TV comedy from 1979. It was a pivotal year for Britain, with a new prime minister in Margaret Thatcher and mass strikes leading to the so-called 'Winter of Discontent'. Television saw a return to political satire, with the BBC series Not the Nine O'Clock News, though the northern club scene was dominating comedic output at the time with the likes of The Cannon & Ball Show and the stand-up series The Comedians. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin are mystified by the murders of a popular couple who were stabbed to death while they slept. Lacking any leads to possible grievances held or enemies made, the detectives suspect a case of mistaken identity, and the previous owner of the house - a banker caught up in a hedge-fund scandal - emerges as the more likely target. Guest starring Samuel West

programme.title: Prime Suspect 1973
programme.desc: Crime drama prequel following the early career of Jane Tennison, as she starts out as a single-minded 22-year-old probationary officer in 1970s London, where chauvinism and rule-bending are the norm. Jane is thrown into a brutal murder inquiry when the body of a teenage girl, Julie-Ann Collins, is found in the underground car park of a Hackney housing estate, while the family of local crime matriarch Renee Bentley are shaken by a violent attack on her. Stefanie Martini steps into the shoes of Helen Mirren to bring the character back to the small screen, with Sam Reid, Blake Harrison and Alun Armstrong also starring

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Ghosts from the past return to haunt Morse as he investigates the apparent suicide of a terminally ill Oxford don whose widow turns out to be the woman the detective was set to marry in his youth. As the case develops, Lewis worries his partner's professionalism is being undermined. Starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Joanna David, Samantha Bond, David Haig, Adrian Dunbar and James Grout

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: On doctor's orders, the dapper Belgian sleuth visits Brighton for what he hopes will be a relaxing holiday, but it leads to one of the most absorbing cases he has ever faced - the disappearance of a cache of jewels. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: Bless This House

programme.title: George and Mildred

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve comes to the aid of an amnesiac struggling to piece together his identity, Jenny tells Dennis she is tired of playing second fiddle to his career, and Bernie and David step in when it appears Vernon's money-making schemes are getting the better of him. Rural drama, starring James Carlton, Ian East, Peter Benson, David Lonsdale and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vic feels bad about causing Tom to lose his job. Charlie is horrified to hear Tony talk about adoption of Emma's baby. The Dingles start their bus service

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: A social worker speaks to Emma about her baby. Terry tries to clear the air with Viv, and she changes her mind about leaving Vic. Rachel gives Chris her divorce papers

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alec plans to put on a talent night. Martin and Gail are stunned when Don asks Nick to be his business partner. Alf announces to the residents that the street is to be renamed

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The receivers take over the garage and go through the books with Don. Kevin is made redundant with two weeks' pay. Maxine confesses to Fiona that she is going out with Tony

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth finds himself in interesting company as English socialites and American millionaires rub shoulders on board a train bound for the French Riviera. Passengers include oil magnate Rufus Van Aldin (Elliott Gould) and his daughter Ruth (Jaime Murray) - to whom he has given a magnificent ruby known as the Heart of Fire. With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve comes to the aid of an amnesiac struggling to piece together his identity, Jenny tells Dennis she is tired of playing second fiddle to his career, and Bernie and David step in when it appears Vernon's money-making schemes are getting the better of him. Rural drama, starring James Carlton, Ian East, Peter Benson, David Lonsdale and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper sleuth returns to his home country of Belgium for the first time since the First World War to attend a medal ceremony, only to find a murder case needs solving. Agatha Christie mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Chancellor and David De Keyser

programme.title: Judi Dench's Wild Borneo Adventure
programme.desc: The actress journeys more than 560km down Borneo's Kinabatangan river and along the island's coastline to explore the unique wildlife that lives in this threatened environment. With scientist Kerisha Kntayya, Judi joins a crocodile hunt with a difference. Kerisha plucks young crocodiles out of the water. Judi then joins Kerisha's team as they wrestle an adult croc as part of the study Kerisha hopes will help save these prehistoric creatures. The Oscar winner, who's had a fascination with bats from an early age, also explores the Gomantong cave, home to more than a million bats

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: Comedy milestones of 1984, when cult hits like The Young Ones and Alas Smith and Jones battled it out in the schedules with more mainstream fare like Russ Abbot's Madhouse, and politicians were targeted by one of the most irreverent TV shows of all time - Spitting Image. Fans also lost two of the greatest comedy stars of all time - Tommy Cooper and Eric Morecambe. Narrated by Hugh Bonneville

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The team struggles to understand the involvement of 12-year-old Kaden Blake in the shooting of mother and carer Lia Brown. As a victim of gang culture, the boy's only protection is keeping silent, and by speaking out about the crime, he sets in motion a devastating chain of events. Guest starring Lucy Speed (EastEnders, The Bill), with Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Prime Suspect 1973
programme.desc: The police delve deeper into the murder of Julie-Ann Collins, setting their sights on the driver of the car the victim was last seen climbing into. However, as the hunt gets under way, relations become strained between Jane and DI Bradfield when she walks her drunken boss home one night. Meanwhile, Clifford Bentley is fuming over the attack on his wife by gangland kingpin Clay Whiteley and launches a revenge attack - putting his family's criminal interests in jeopardy. Stefanie Martini stars as the novice WPC who would eventually become the tough senior detective made famous by Helen Mirren, with Sam Reid and Alun Armstrong

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a well-known business tycoon leads Morse to investigate the man's feuding family, and inadvertently makes him the unwitting target of a hate campaign by the tabloids - a problem compounded by his uneasy relationship with a new chief superintendent. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star alongside Gwen Taylor, Martin Clunes, Alun Armstrong, Anna Massey, Charlotte Coleman and Rupert Graves

programme.title: World of Sport
programme.desc: Richard Henwood presents action from obscure motorsports, such as bus and caravan racing and ice speedway

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: When a conman and his girlfriend trick a prominent scientist with a tale about hidden treasure, he is taken hostage in Hamburg and sold to the Russians. Templar is 'lured' along similar lines and the confidence trick enables the spy to find the missing man. Roger Moore stars

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A distinguished Army colonel is found dead in mysterious circumstances and the finger of suspicion points to his widow. Holmes has to solve a murder involving a locked room with no key, a domestic quarrel and odd animal-like tracks outside the window. Drama, starring Jeremy Brett and David Burke

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: The duo investigate the strange deaths of several astronomers whose bodies have been mysteriously bleached white, and encounter an eccentric society dedicated to establishing contact with aliens from Venus. Jon Pertwee guest stars in the series' first colour episode

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Private investigator Caroline Selby seeks Arthur's help in locating eccentric recluse and inventor Albert Goddard, who has come into an inheritance. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 uncovers a plan to hold the home secretary hostage in order to demand the release of a dangerous prisoner. Cowley takes the politician's place at an award ceremony, hoping that with Bodie as his bodyguard, he can draw the enemy into the open and thwart the attempt. Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Gordon Jackson and Lewis Collins, and featuring Pamela Stephenson in an early role

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Two former cellmates attempt to throw a suspicious Regan off their trail by pretending that they have fallen out and that one has murdered the other. Maureen Lipman guests stars, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar investigates allegations that the Loch Ness monster not only exists, but is also responsible for a mysterious spate of killings in Scotland. Fulton Mackay guest stars alongside Roger Moore

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A woman hires Holmes and Watson to investigate the strange death of her sister, whose enigmatic final words seemingly hold the key to the case. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett and David Burke

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed and Mrs Peel investigate a spate of murders, and discover a ruthless businessman has been killing his rivals to gain a competitive edge. Vintage adventures, starring Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Terry panics after putting himself in an incriminating position by leaving his fingerprints on a stolen lorry full of electrical items he has collected for Arthur. George Cole and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: An expert sniper with a high-powered imported rifle holds a family hostage in a high-rise flat, planning to target a royal visitor at the Wimbledon tennis tournament. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Suspected felon Billy Medhurst returns from Spain, and Regan determines to prove his involvement in an earlier robbery in which a Sweeney office was shot and left permanently disabled. Police drama, starring John Thaw, Dennis Waterman and Anthony Heaton

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five family members from Lincoln answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: On a remote lake in Malaysia, two men mysteriously vanish - just hours apart. Amid rumours that a giant fish is on the loose, biologist and monster-hunter Jeremy Wade sets out to track down the possible culprit. Could the killer be an interloper from South America, as reports suggest, or is there a home-grown predator that fits the bill? A trail of evidence leads Jeremy into an ancient rainforest, where an old adversary lies in wait. More than just a beast with a gruesome rap sheet, it's also the one river monster that he has thus far failed to catch

programme.title: Manhunter (1986)
programme.desc: A former FBI agent is coaxed out of retirement to track down a serial killer behind the slaughter of entire families. The detective needs an angle on the murderer's psychology and the only way to get this insight is to face his nightmares and consult cannibalistic psychopath Dr Hannibal Lecktor in prison. Michael Mann's thriller, based on Thomas Harris's novel Red Dragon, with William Petersen, Dennis Farina, Tom Noonan, Brian Cox and Joan Allen

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: Latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.desc: A downbeat cop sets out to escort a prostitute across America so she can give evidence in a high-profile Mob trial. Meanwhile, the mismatched duo's bid to reach their destination alive is complicated by a burgeoning romance. Action adventure, directed by and starring Clint Eastwood. With Sondra Locke, Bill McKinney and Pat Hingle

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar investigates the murder of a journalist friend, following a trail of clues that leads to a cult obsessed with the splendour and savagery of ancient Rome. Drama, guest starring Peter Wyngarde and Ed Bishop

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A governess takes up a new position, but feels constrained to consult Holmes about some of the bizarre conditions stipulated by her employer. Period crime drama, guest starring Natasha Richardson, Joss Ackland and Patience Collier, with Jeremy Brett and David Burke

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: When the Ministry is broken into by an apparently invisible man, it seems a secret formula has fallen into the hands of an organisation run by Soviet Intelligence. Light-hearted action, starring Patrick Macnee and Diana Rigg

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur becomes involved with a gambler, whose sudden death leaves the dodgy car dealer £2,000 worse off. Meanwhile, Terry helps his old flame Sylvie leave her abusive husband. Billy Connolly guest stars in the vintage comedy drama, alongside George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Cowley and the team present a line of defence against groups of mercenaries and guerrillas who are waging war on the streets of Britain. David Suchet and Geoffrey Palmer guest star alongside Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson

programme.title: Golf: PGA Tour Highlights
programme.desc: Extended highlights of The Northern Trust tournament from Boston, as the world's leading golfers met in the first of three FedExCup Playoff events, culminating in the Tour Championship at the start of September. A prize purse of $9.5million was up for grabs, with Patrick Reed defending the title he won 12 months ago

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade embarks on one of his most challenging journeys as he investigates reports of a face-eating creature in a remote part of Bolivia. Finding his skills and knowledge tested to the limit, he encounters one of his oldest adversaries and discovers how a species can adapt its behaviour to survive

programme.title: British Superbike Championship Highlights
programme.desc: James Haydon presents action from the second round of the campaign, which came from Snetterton. Scott Redding was the standout performer at the 2019 race staged on this circuit, as he took the chequered flag in both races. The Englishman won 11 races overall last season, as he went on to win the riders' championship by just five points over Australia's Josh Brookes

programme.title: King of the Roads
programme.desc: The Barry Sheene Classic Festival, held annually at England's only closed road race - Oliver's Mount in Scarborough. The event is named after two-time 500cc world champion Barry Sheene, who contested at the parkland circuit for over 15 years and won the feature race four times

programme.title: Sudden Death (1995)
programme.desc: Terrorists seize control of an ice-hockey stadium in the middle of a match attended by the US vice president. Taking the politician hostage, they threaten to blow up the building at the final whistle - but a resourceful former fireman takes it upon himself to thwart their dastardly plot. Action thriller, starring Jean-Claude Van Damme, Powers Boothe, Raymond J Barry and Whittni Wright

programme.desc: Latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.desc: Terrorists seize control of an ice-hockey stadium in the middle of a match attended by the US vice president. programme.title: Manhunter (1986)
programme.desc: A former FBI agent is coaxed out of retirement to track down a serial killer behind the slaughter of entire families. The detective needs an angle on the murderer's psychology and the only way to get this insight is to face his nightmares and consult cannibalistic psychopath Dr Hannibal Lecktor in prison. Michael Mann's thriller, based on Thomas Harris's novel Red Dragon, with William Petersen, Dennis Farina, Tom Noonan, Brian Cox and Joan Allen

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: When a conman and his girlfriend trick a prominent scientist with a tale about hidden treasure, he is taken hostage in Hamburg and sold to the Russians. Templar is 'lured' along similar lines and the confidence trick enables the spy to find the missing man. Roger Moore stars

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A distinguished Army colonel is found dead in mysterious circumstances and the finger of suspicion points to his widow. Holmes has to solve a murder involving a locked room with no key, a domestic quarrel and odd animal-like tracks outside the window. Drama, starring Jeremy Brett and David Burke

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: The duo investigate the strange deaths of several astronomers whose bodies have been mysteriously bleached white, and encounter an eccentric society dedicated to establishing contact with aliens from Venus. Jon Pertwee guest stars in the series' first colour episode

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Private investigator Caroline Selby seeks Arthur's help in locating eccentric recluse and inventor Albert Goddard, who has come into an inheritance. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 uncovers a plan to hold the home secretary hostage in order to demand the release of a dangerous prisoner. Cowley takes the politician's place at an award ceremony, hoping that with Bodie as his bodyguard, he can draw the enemy into the open and thwart the attempt. Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Gordon Jackson and Lewis Collins, and featuring Pamela Stephenson in an early role

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Two former cellmates attempt to throw a suspicious Regan off their trail by pretending that they have fallen out and that one has murdered the other. Maureen Lipman guests stars, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar investigates allegations that the Loch Ness monster not only exists, but is also responsible for a mysterious spate of killings in Scotland. Fulton Mackay guest stars alongside Roger Moore

programme.title: The Sweeney

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five family members from Lincoln answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: On a remote lake in Malaysia, two men mysteriously vanish - just hours apart. Amid rumours that a giant fish is on the loose, biologist and monster-hunter Jeremy Wade sets out to track down the possible culprit. Could the killer be an interloper from South America, as reports suggest, or is there a home-grown predator that fits the bill? A trail of evidence leads Jeremy into an ancient rainforest, where an old adversary lies in wait. More than just a beast with a gruesome rap sheet, it's also the one river monster that he has thus far failed to catch

Michael Mann's thriller, based on Thomas Harris's novel Red Dragon, with William Petersen, Dennis Farina, Tom Noonan, Brian Cox and Joan Allen

The detective needs an angle on the murderer's psychology and the only way to get this insight is to face his nightmares and consult cannibalistic psychopath Dr Hannibal Lecktor in prison. Michael Mann's thriller, based on Thomas Harris's novel Red Dragon, with William Petersen, Dennis Farina, Tom Noonan, Brian Cox and Joan Allen

programme.desc: Kat Cole goes undercover among the workforce of her company Cinnabon Incorporated, which serves sweet treats from more than 900 bakeries in 40 countries worldwide. She seeks advice from her old boss, former Hooters CEO Coby Brooks, who also went undercover, in his own organisation. There is also advice on how to make money selling clothes online and a look at the popular 'buy now, pay later' schemes offered by fashion retailers

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking Family Favourites
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver puts his twist on cauliflower cheese as he teams it with spaghetti to create a creamy, silky smooth pasta dish topped off with golden crunchy breadcrumbs. Reinventing another family favourite, Jamie offers a healthier take on the classic chicken Kiev, wrapping the meat in filo pastry instead of breadcrumbs to create light, crispy parcels, while the chef introduces his son Buddy to Scotch bonnet chillies in a spicy barbecue chicken burger, with a tip to manage the heat to suit any member of the family

programme.title: The Yorkshire Jobcentre
programme.desc: The team of job coaches gives advice to a 58-year-old recently made redundant from his job in IT, where he'd worked for nearly 20 years. A single mother from the Czech Republic who has recently moved to Leeds following a break-up applies for universal credit, but has to take a residence test to determine whether she will be eligible for anything

programme.title: Swingers
programme.desc: Documentary about the scene in which people engage in group sex or swap sexual partners, going behind the scenes at one of Britain's most popular swingers' clubs to find out what kinds of people attend and why

programme.title: Crime and Punishment
programme.desc: With more than 700 children currently serving prison sentences across England and Wales, the documentary asks why Britain struggles to stop young offenders being sucked into a life of crime. DS Will Whale and his team at Southampton CID investigate 25 burglaries from elderly people's homes, crimes they suspect are connected to a group of teenagers. In Winchester Prison, a young inmate is sent to the segregation unit after climbing onto the roof, while in Hampshire, a probation officer is doing everything she can to keep a prolific offender on the straight and narrow

programme.title: Lose a Stone in 21 Days with Michael Mosley
programme.desc: The doctor puts five volunteers on a low-calorie diet to help get their health back on track after the UK's Covid-19 lockdown. In the third and final week of the diet challenge, Michael explores the connection between comfort eating, and the different ways our brains respond when faced with a bar of chocolate as opposed to a pile of fruit and vegetables. He also has the unique experience of having a camera stuck up his nose to learn about the connection between weight, snoring and the life-limiting condition of sleep apnoea

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Atlanta, Georgia, in an attempt to save a family run smokehouse restaurant plagued by mismanagement and lazy cooking. Michon's owner, Al Wilson, had always hoped he could pass his beloved business on to daughter Natalie, but when he suffered a collapsed lung the handover came about much earlier than expected. Now it's down to Gordon to motivate Natalie and save Michon's from disaster

programme.desc: Four teenagers and their parents struggle to deal with the complicated ways in which modern technology has affected their lives and how they communicate. programme.desc: The broadcaster examines how trains transformed the nation and shaped modern Britain. Chris also tells the story of Henry Booth, who not only championed George Stephenson's famous Rocket, but also helped finance it, and talks about the work of the navvies who dug the tunnels and laid the tracks

Amanda seeks to document their daily lives under lockdown with a video diary, enlisting the kids as camera crew - which results in an unusual toddlers' eye view of the farm. As the temperature rises in the spring, the family takes advantage of the good conditions to go horse riding and wild swimming

programme.title: Grace of Monaco: Hollywood Princess
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the life of Grace Kelly, examining her transition from Oscar-winning Hollywood star to marrying into one of the world's most closely observed royal families. The programme examines her feelings concerning this change, questioning if she ever longed to return to the screen. Featuring rarely seen archive footage and insider interviews

programme.title: The Murder of Marilyn Monroe
programme.desc: Investigation into the star's state of mind in the weeks immediately prior to her death, examining her troubled personal life and relationship with psychiatrist Dr Ralph Greenson. Friends, photographers, press agents and doctors challenge the conventional wisdom that she committed suicide, and a variety of alternative scenarios are considered

programme.title: The Railways That Built Britain with Chris Tarrant
programme.desc: The broadcaster examines the role of the railways during the two global conflicts of the 20th century, keeping vital supply lines open and transporting troops to and from the front. In the First World War, railway works were converted into munitions factories and women were employed for these dangerous jobs and their heroic efforts helped win them the vote, while in the Second World War, they were vital in organising the evacuation of a million children and offered underground shelters from air raids

programme.title: Walking Britain's Lost Railways
programme.desc: Rob Bell explores the Waverley Route, which ran south from Edinburgh for 100 miles through the Scottish Borders to Carlisle, connecting with what is now known as the West Coast Main Line. After years of campaigning, the first stage of Rob's journey is aboard the new Borders Railway, the longest railway to open in over a century. It follows part of the old Waverley Route, making fine use of the 170-year-old, 23-arch Newbattle Viaduct. Rob also pays a visit to one of Hawick's surviving cashmere factories and discovers a First World War prisoner of war camp served by the railway

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Derbyshire officers target violent gangs ram-raiding cash machines, with criminals using hydraulic power cutters and saws to cut the ATMs out, gas pumped into the cash slots to blow them up, or heavy construction vehicles to smash them out of the walls. Meanwhile, Sgt Adrian is called to a report of an attempted burglary and Vicky is alerted to a suspected stolen car with fake plates on the M1

programme.title: Police Code Zero: Officer Under Attack
programme.desc: Programme exploring the rising number of assaults on police officers, revealing what they face in the line of duty, and the emotional toll these incidents take. A late-night patrol pulls over a man suspected of driving under the influence, but they soon find themselves dealing with much more. Meanwhile, officers responded to an emergency call by residents on an estate, but the atmosphere takes a worrying turn when the residents turn on the officers

programme.title: Cannibals & Killers: Inside the World's Toughest Prisons
programme.desc: Documentary offering a never-before seen look inside three high-security Russian prisons. Cameras go inside Europe's oldest operating jail, Vladimir Central, Russia's largest prison with more than 1,200 inmates, and one of its convicts opens up about killing his brother-in-law for disturbing his daughter's sleep. Cameras also explore the Black Dolphin prison, near the Kazakhstan border, which is home to some of the highest category offenders in the world, and a frozen Siberian prison camp, where two inmates go their separate ways as one gets parole, while the other does not

programme.title: The Sex Business
programme.desc: Documentary taking a look at how people buy, sell and market sex. A disparate group of relatively new recruits to Britain's flourishing and fast-growing porn industry reveals what life is really like when trying to make a living in the adult movie industry

programme.title: Good Girls' Guide to Kinky Sex
programme.desc: The bedroom experiment continues as three self-confessed good girls and their guys look for creative ways to improve their sex lives. They experiment with temperature play, discovering ways of turning their skin and senses wild with ice cubes and lubricants, before trying out a selection of sex toys. Finally they try a little role play by acting out their partner's ultimate fantasy for the night
